title.raw: 卷十九 耿弇列傳 Volume 19: Biography of Geng Yan

sentences.zh: 耿弇字伯昭，扶風茂陵人也。

translations.literal: Geng Yan, styled Bozhao, was a man of Maoling in Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan, courtesy name Bozhao, was a native of Maoling in Fufeng commandery.

sentences.zh: 其先武帝時以吏二千石自巨鹿徙焉。

translations.literal: His ancestors, in the time of Emperor Wu, as officials at the rank of two thousand shi, moved from Julu to there.
translations.idiomatic: His forebears had held a two-thousand-dan post under Emperor Wu and relocated from Julu to Maoling.

sentences.zh: 父況，字俠遊，以明經為郎，與王莽從弟伋共學《老子》於安丘先生，後為朔調連率。

translations.literal: His father Kuang, styled Xiayou, through understanding the classics became a gentleman-attendant, and with Wang Mang's cousin Ji together studied the Laozi under Master Anqiu, afterward became chain commandant of Shuodiao.
translations.idiomatic: His father Geng Kuang, courtesy name Xiayou, entered court as a gentleman-attendant on the strength of his classical learning; with Wang Mang's cousin Wang Ji he studied the Laozi under Master Anqiu, and was later appointed chain commandant of the Shuodiao circuit.

sentences.zh: 弇少好學，習父業。

translations.literal: Yan in youth loved study, practiced his father's occupation.
translations.idiomatic: From boyhood Geng Yan loved learning and followed his father's scholarly path.

sentences.zh: 常見郡尉試騎士，建旗鼓，肄馳射，由是好將帥之事。

translations.literal: He often saw the commandery commandant test mounted knights, set up drums and banners, drill mounted archery, thereby came to love generals' and commanders' affairs.
translations.idiomatic: He often watched the commandery commandant drill mounted archers—drums and banners raised, bows shot at full gallop—and so grew fond of the business of command.

sentences.zh: 及王莽敗，更始立，諸將略地者，前後多擅威權，輒改易守、令。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang was defeated, Gengshi was established, the generals who seized territory, before and after, mostly arrogated authority and power, repeatedly changed and replaced governors and magistrates.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang fell and the Gengshi Emperor took the throne, generals who carved out territory one after another seized power at will, deposing governors and magistrates as they pleased.

sentences.zh: 況自以莽之所置，懷不自安。

translations.literal: Kuang himself considered that he was one Mang had installed, harbored not self-peace.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kuang knew he had been appointed by Wang Mang and lived in fear for his position.

sentences.zh: 時，弇年二十一，乃辭況奉奏詣更始，因賫貢獻，以求自固之宜。

translations.literal: At that time, Yan's age was twenty-one, then he took leave of Kuang to present a memorial and go to Gengshi, thereby carrying tribute offerings, to seek what was fitting for self-consolidation.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan was twenty-one. He left his father to deliver a memorial and tribute to the Gengshi court, hoping to secure the family's standing.

sentences.zh: 及至宋子，會王郎詐稱成帝子子輿，起兵邯鄲，弇從吏孫倉、衛包於道共謀曰：『劉子輿成帝正統，舍此不歸，遠行安之？

translations.literal: When he reached Songzi, it happened that Wang Lang falsely claimed to be Emperor Cheng's son Ziyu, raised troops at Handan, Yan's attendant clerks Sun Cang and Wei Bao on the road together plotted saying: 'Liu Ziyu is Emperor Cheng's legitimate line, abandoning this not returning, far traveling where peace?
translations.idiomatic: At Songzi, Wang Lang had risen at Handan, pretending to be Liu Ziyu, son of Emperor Cheng. Two of Geng Yan's clerks, Sun Cang and Wei Bao, waylaid him on the road: 'Liu Ziyu is the true imperial line. Why press on to the capital when we could join him instead?'

sentences.zh: 』弇按劍曰：『子輿弊賊，卒為降虜耳。

translations.literal: ' Geng Yan grasped his sword and said: 'Ziyu is a vile bandit; in the end he will become a surrendered captive only.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan gripped his sword. 'That Liu Ziyu is a spent rebel,' he said. 'He will end his days a prisoner in someone else's camp.'

sentences.zh: 我至長安，與國家陳漁陽、上谷兵馬之用，還出太原、代郡，反復數十日，歸發突騎以轔烏合之眾，如摧枯折腐耳。

translations.literal: I arriving at Chang'an, for the state present the use of Yuyang and Shanggu horses and troops, return out through Taiyuan and Dai commandery, back and forth several tens of days, return dispatch sudden-riding to crush crow-assembled masses, like destroying dry breaking rotten only.
translations.idiomatic: Once I reach Chang'an I will lay before the court how the armies of Yuyang and Shanggu can be used; I will swing back through Taiyuan and Dai, be gone only a few dozen days, then return to raise our shock cavalry and ride down Wang Lang's rabble as easily as snapping dead wood.'

sentences.zh: 觀公等不識去就，族滅不久也！

translations.literal: Observe you gentlemen not knowing go-come, clan extermination not long!
translations.idiomatic: Mark my words: you cannot tell friend from foe, and your whole clans will not long survive it.'

sentences.zh: 』倉、包不從，遂亡降王郎。

translations.literal: ' Cang and Bao did not follow, then fled and surrendered to Wang Lang.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Cang and Wei Bao would not listen; they deserted and went over to Wang Lang.

sentences.zh: 弇道聞光武在盧奴，乃馳北上謁，光武留署門下吏。

translations.literal: Yan on the road heard Guangwu was at Lunu, then galloped north to go pay court, Guangwu retained him to serve as a gate clerk.
translations.idiomatic: On the road Geng Yan learned that Guangwu was at Lunu; he spurred north to join him, and Guangwu kept him on as a clerk of the outer gate.

sentences.zh: 弇因說護軍朱祐，求歸發兵，以定邯鄲。

translations.literal: Yan thereby persuaded protector-of-the-army Zhu You, sought return to dispatch troops, in order to settle Handan.
translations.idiomatic: He persuaded the protector of the army, Zhu You, to let him go home and raise troops to crush Wang Lang at Handan.

sentences.zh: 光武笑曰：『小兒曹乃有大意哉！

translations.literal: Guangwu laughed saying: 'Little boys band actually have great intention!
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu laughed. 'What lofty designs for a pack of youngsters!'

sentences.zh: 』因數召見加恩慰。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon repeatedly summoned him to audience, added favor and comfort.
translations.idiomatic: After that he summoned Geng Yan often, showed him marked favor, and put him at ease.

sentences.zh: 弇因從光武北至薊。

translations.literal: Yan thereby followed Guangwu north to Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan then accompanied Guangwu north as far as Ji.

sentences.zh: 聞邯鄲兵方到，光武將欲南歸，召官屬計議。

translations.literal: Hearing Handan troops just arrived, Guangwu generals desired south return, summoned officials and subordinates to plan and discuss.
translations.idiomatic: When word came that Wang Lang's army from Handan was almost upon them, Guangwu's officers wanted to withdraw south; he called his staff to council.

sentences.zh: 弇曰：『今兵從南來，不可南行。

translations.literal: Yan said: 'Now troops from south come, not permissible south go.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan said, 'The enemy is closing from the south; we cannot march south into them.'

sentences.zh: 漁陽太守彭寵，公之邑人；

translations.literal: Yuyang governor Peng Chong, is the lord's same-town man;
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong, governor of Yuyang, is a townsman of yours;

sentences.zh: 上谷太守，即弇父也。

translations.literal: Shanggu governor, is Yan's father.
translations.idiomatic: the governor of Shanggu is my own father.'

sentences.zh: 發此兩郡，控弦萬騎，邯鄲不足慮也。

translations.literal: Dispatch these two commanderies, bow-string ten thousand horse, Handan not sufficient worry.
translations.idiomatic: Call out both commanderies and you have ten thousand mounted archers at your back—Handan need not keep you awake at night.'

sentences.zh: 』光武官屬腹心皆不肯，曰：『死尚南首，奈何北行入囊中？

translations.literal: ' Guangwu's officials and trusted confidants all were not willing, saying: 'Death still south head, how north go enter in bag?
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu's closest advisers would have none of it. 'Even in death we face south,' they said. 'How can we ride north straight into the trap?'

sentences.zh: 』光武指弇曰：『是我北道主人也。

translations.literal: ' Guangwu pointed at Yan saying: 'This is my north-road host-person.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu pointed at Geng Yan. 'He is my host on the northern road.'

sentences.zh: 』會薊中亂，光武遂南馳，官屬各分散。

translations.literal: ' It happened Ji within was in chaos, Guangwu thereupon galloped south, officials and subordinates each scattered.
translations.idiomatic: Then rioting broke out in Ji; Guangwu spurred south while his staff scattered in every direction.

sentences.zh: 弇走昌平就況，因說況使寇恂東約彭寵，各發突騎二千匹，步兵千人。

translations.literal: Yan fled to Changping to join Kuang, thereby persuaded Kuang to send Kou Xun east to covenant with Peng Chong, each dispatched sudden-riding two thousand horses, foot soldiers one thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan fled to Changping to rejoin his father and persuaded Geng Kuang to send Kou Xun east to treat with Peng Chong; each commandery raised two thousand mounted shock troops and a thousand foot soldiers.

sentences.zh: 弇與景丹、寇恂及漁陽兵合軍而南，所過擊斬王郎大將、九卿、校尉以下四百餘級，得印綬百二十五，節二，斬首三萬級，定涿郡、中山、巨鹿、清河、河間凡二十二縣，遂及光武於廣阿。

translations.literal: Yan with Jing Dan, Kou Xun and Yuyang troops combined army and south, where passed struck and beheaded Wang Lang great generals, nine ministers, colonels and below more than four hundred grades, obtained seals and ribbons one hundred twenty-five, tallies two, beheaded heads thirty thousand grades, settled Zhuo, Zhongshan, Julu, Qinghe, Hejian altogether twenty-two counties, thereupon caught up with Guangwu at Guang'a.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan, Jing Dan, and Kou Xun joined the Yuyang contingent and marched south, killing Wang Lang's generals, ministers of state, colonels, and lower officers—over four hundred in rank—along the way. They seized 125 official seals, two credentials of authority, and 30,000 heads. They secured twenty-two counties in Zhuo, Zhongshan, Julu, Qinghe, and Hejian, then overtook Guangwu at Guang'a.

sentences.zh: 是時，光武方攻王郎，傳言二郡兵為邯鄲來，眾皆恐。

translations.literal: At this time, Guangwu was attacking Wang Lang, transmitted words said the two commanderies' troops for Handan came, the masses all feared.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was still besieging Wang Lang when rumor spread that the two northern armies were marching for Handan against him; his men were terrified.

sentences.zh: 既而悉詣營上謁。

translations.literal: Afterward all went to camp to pay court.
translations.idiomatic: In the event they all rode into camp to present themselves.

sentences.zh: 光武見弇等，說，曰：『當與漁陽、上谷士大夫共此大功。

translations.literal: Guangwu saw Yan and others, pleased, said: 'Ought with Yuyang and Shanggu scholar-officials together this great merit.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu saw Geng Yan and his comrades he was delighted. 'I shall share this great achievement,' he said, 'with the gentlemen of Yuyang and Shanggu.'

sentences.zh: 』乃皆以為偏將軍，使還領其兵。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon all made them lieutenant-generals, ordered return to lead their troops.
translations.idiomatic: He appointed them all lieutenant-generals and sent them back to command their own troops.

sentences.zh: 加況大將軍、興義侯，得自置偏裨。

translations.literal: Added Kuang great general, Xingyi marquis, obtained self appoint lieutenant and adjutant.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kuang was promoted to grand general and marquis of Xingyi, with authority to appoint his own subordinate commanders.

sentences.zh: 弇等遂從拔邯鄲。

translations.literal: Yan and others thereupon followed and captured Handan.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan and the others then took part in the capture of Handan.

sentences.zh: 時，更始征代郡太守趙永，而況勸永不應召，令詣於光武。

translations.literal: At that time, Gengshi summoned Dai commandery governor Zhao Yong, but Kuang urged Yong not to respond to the summons, ordered him to go to Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: The Gengshi court summoned Zhao Yong, governor of Dai, but Geng Kuang persuaded him to ignore the call and report instead to Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 光武遣永復郡。

translations.literal: Guangwu dispatched Yong to restore the commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent Zhao Yong back to take charge of the commandery.

sentences.zh: 永北還，而代令張曄據城反畔，乃招迎匈奴、烏桓以為援助。

translations.literal: Yong returned north, but Dai magistrate Zhang Ye seized the city and rebelled, then summoned and welcomed the Xiongnu and Wuhuan as aid.
translations.idiomatic: On the way north the magistrate of Dai, Zhang Ye, seized the city in revolt and called in the Xiongnu and Wuhuan for support.

sentences.zh: 光武以弇弟舒為復胡將軍，使擊曄，破之。

translations.literal: Guangwu made Yan's younger brother Shu general who restores the Hu, ordered attack Ye, defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu appointed Geng Yan's brother Geng Shu general for the pacification of the Hu and sent him against Zhang Ye, who was crushed.

sentences.zh: 永乃得復郡。

translations.literal: Yong thereby was able to restore the commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Yong was then able to resume his governorship.

sentences.zh: 時，五校賊二十餘萬北寇上谷，況與舒連擊破之，賊皆退走。

translations.literal: At that time, Wuxiao bandits more than two hundred thousand north raided Shanggu, Kuang with Shu continuously struck and defeated them, bandits all retreated and fled.
translations.idiomatic: More than two hundred thousand troops of the Wuxiao bands invaded Shanggu from the north; Geng Kuang and Geng Shu drove them off in succession until the rebels withdrew.

sentences.zh: 更始見光武威聲日盛，君臣疑慮，乃遣使立光武為蕭王，令罷兵與諸將有功者還長安；

translations.literal: Gengshi saw Guangwu's martial sound day flourish, ruler and ministers doubted and hesitated, then dispatched envoys to establish Guangwu as Prince of Xiao, ordered dismiss troops with various generals who had merit return to Chang'an;
translations.idiomatic: As Guangwu's renown grew daily, the Gengshi court grew uneasy. It sent envoys to enfeoff him as Prince of Xiao and ordered him to disband his army and return to Chang'an with his meritorious generals;

sentences.zh: 遣苗曾為幽州牧，韋順為上谷太守，蔡充為漁陽太守，並北之部。

translations.literal: dispatched Miao Zeng as Youzhou shepherd, Wei Shun as Shanggu governor, Cai Chong as Yuyang governor, together north to their posts.
translations.idiomatic: it also appointed Miao Zeng governor of Youzhou, Wei Shun governor of Shanggu, and Cai Chong governor of Yuyang, all bound for their northern jurisdictions.

sentences.zh: 時，光武居邯鄲宮，晝臥溫明殿。

translations.literal: At that time, Guangwu dwelt Handan palace, day slept Warm Bright Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was lodged in the Handan palace, napping by day in the Warm Bright Hall.

sentences.zh: 弇入造床下請間，因說曰：『今更始失政，君臣淫亂，諸將擅命於畿內，貴戚縱橫於都內。

translations.literal: Yan entered went under the bed requesting private moment, thereby spoke saying: 'Now Gengshi loses government, ruler and ministers licentious and chaotic, various generals arrogated orders within the capital region, noble kin run wild within the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan slipped in beneath the couch to beg a private word. 'The Gengshi regime has lost its grip,' he said. 'Court and ministers wallow in vice; generals issue their own orders inside the capital districts; great clans ride roughshod through the city.'

sentences.zh: 天子之命，不出城門，所在牧守，輒自遷易，百姓不知所從，士人莫敢自安。

translations.literal: Son of Heaven's commands, not exit city gates, wherever shepherd-governors, repeatedly self transfer and change, hundred surnames know not what follow, scholar-officials none dare self peace.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial decrees never leave the palace gates; governors everywhere are replaced at will. The people do not know whom to obey, and no gentleman feels safe in his post.'

sentences.zh: 虜掠財物，劫掠婦女，懷金玉者，至不生歸。

translations.literal: Capture plunder goods, rob plunder women, those who harbor gold and jade, to not alive return.
translations.idiomatic: They loot property, seize women, and anyone who hoards gold and jade is lucky to leave with his life.'

sentences.zh: 元元叩心，更思莽朝。

translations.literal: Common folk knock heart, further think Mang court.
translations.idiomatic: The common people beat their breasts and think back longingly even to Wang Mang's day.'

sentences.zh: 又銅馬、赤眉之屬數十輩，輩數十百萬，聖公不能辦也。

translations.literal: Moreover Bronze Horse, Red Eyebrows' type several tens bands, bands several tens hundred thousand, Sheng Gong cannot manage.
translations.idiomatic: Besides, there are dozens of hosts like the Bronze Horse and the Red Eyebrows, each numbering hundreds of thousands—Liu Xuan cannot possibly cope.'

sentences.zh: 其敗不久，公首事南陽，破百萬之軍；

translations.literal: Their defeat not long, lord first raised affairs Nanyang, broke million's army;
translations.idiomatic: Their fall cannot be far off. You first raised the standard at Nanyang and shattered armies counted in the millions;

sentences.zh: 今定河北，據天府之地。

translations.literal: now settled Hebei, occupy heaven-storehouse land.
translations.idiomatic: you have secured Hebei and hold the storehouse of the realm.'

sentences.zh: 以義征伐，發號響應，天下可傳檄而定。

translations.literal: With righteousness campaign and punish, issue commands echo respond, under Heaven can transmit proclamation and settle.
translations.idiomatic: Campaign in the name of right, and the empire will answer your call; the realm can be settled with a single manifesto.'

sentences.zh: 天下至重，不可令它姓得之。

translations.literal: Under Heaven utmost heavy, not permissible order other surname obtain it.
translations.idiomatic: The mandate is too weighty to be surrendered to some other house.'

sentences.zh: 聞使者從西方來，欲罷兵，不可從也。

translations.literal: Hear envoys from west come, desire dismiss troops, not permissible follow.
translations.idiomatic: I hear envoys from the west are coming to order you to stand down—do not obey them.'

sentences.zh: 今吏士死亡者多，弇願歸幽州，益發精兵，以集大計。

translations.literal: Now clerks and soldiers death many, Yan wishes return Youzhou, increase dispatch crack troops, in order to gather great plan.
translations.idiomatic: Many of your men have fallen. I ask leave to return to Youzhou and raise more picked troops for the great design ahead.'

sentences.zh: 』光武大說，乃拜弇為大將軍，與吳漢北發幽州十郡兵。

translations.literal: ' Guangwu greatly pleased, thereupon appointed Yan as great general, with Wu Han north dispatch Youzhou ten commanderies' troops.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was overjoyed. He appointed Geng Yan grand general and, with Wu Han, sent him north to levy the ten commanderies of Youzhou.

sentences.zh: 弇到上谷，收韋順、蔡充斬之；

translations.literal: Yan arrived Shanggu, seized Wei Shun, Cai Chong beheaded them;
translations.idiomatic: At Shanggu, Geng Yan seized Wei Shun and Cai Chong and had them executed;

sentences.zh: 漢亦誅苗曾。

translations.literal: Han also executed Miao Zeng.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han likewise put Miao Zeng to death.

sentences.zh: 於是悉發幽州兵，引而南，從光武擊破銅馬、高湖、赤眉、青犢，又追尤來、大槍、五幡於元氏，弇常將精騎為軍鋒，輒破走之。

translations.literal: Thereupon fully dispatched Youzhou troops, led and south, followed Guangwu struck and defeated Bronze Horse, High Lake, Red Eyebrows, Green Calf, again pursued Youlai, Great Lance, Five Pennants at Yuanshi, Yan always led crack cavalry as army point, repeatedly defeated and routed them.
translations.idiomatic: The full strength of Youzhou marched south with Guangwu. They broke the Bronze Horse, the High Lake, the Red Eyebrows, and the Green Calf bands, then ran down the Youlai, Great Lance, and Five Pennants at Yuanshi. Geng Yan habitually led elite cavalry as the vanguard and drove the enemy from the field every time.

sentences.zh: 光武乘勝戰順水上，虜危急，殊死戰。

translations.literal: Guangwu riding victory battled Shunshui above, enemy critical peril, fight-to-death battle.
translations.idiomatic: Pressing his advantage, Guangwu gave battle on the Shun River; the rebels, cornered, fought to the death.

sentences.zh: 時，軍士疲弊，遂大敗奔還，壁范陽，數日乃振，賊亦退去，從追至容城、小廣陽、安次，連戰破之。

translations.literal: At that time, soldiers weary exhausted, thereupon great defeat ran returned, walled Fanyang, several days then recovered, bandits also retreated withdrew, followed pursued to Rongcheng, Little Guangyang, Anci, continuous battles defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: His troops were spent and broke in panic, falling back to fortify Fanyang; only after several days did they rally. The rebels withdrew as well, and Guangwu pursued them through Rongcheng, Lesser Guangyang, and Anci, defeating them in a series of engagements.

sentences.zh: 光武還薊，復遣弇與吳漢、景丹、蓋延、朱祐、邳彤、耿純、劉植、岑彭、祭遵、堅鐔、王霸、陳俊、馬武十三將軍，追賊至潞東，及平谷，再戰，斬首萬三千餘級，遂窮追於右北平無終、土垠之間，至俊靡而還。

translations.literal: Guangwu returned Ji, again dispatched Yan with Wu Han, Jing Dan, Gai Yan, Zhu You, Pi Tong, Geng Chun, Liu Zhi, Cen Peng, Ji Zun, Jian Tan, Wang Ba, Chen Jun, Ma Wu thirteen generals, pursued bandits to Lu east, reached Pinggu, again battled, beheaded heads more than thirteen thousand grades, thereupon exhaust pursued at Right Beiping between Wuzhong and Tuyin, reached Junmi and returned.
translations.idiomatic: From Ji, Guangwu sent Geng Yan with Wu Han, Jing Dan, Gai Yan, Zhu You, Pi Tong, Geng Chun, Liu Zhi, Cen Peng, Ji Zun, Jian Tan, Wang Ba, Chen Jun, and Ma Wu—thirteen generals in all—to pursue the enemy east of Lu and on to Pinggu. In a second battle they took more than thirteen thousand heads, then pressed the chase through eastern Right Beiping between Wuzhong and Tuyin as far as Junmi before turning back.

sentences.zh: 賊散入遼西、遼東，或為烏桓、貊人所抄擊，略盡。

translations.literal: Bandits scattered entered Liaoxi, Liaodong, some by Wuhuan, Mo people raided and struck, plundered exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: The remnants scattered into Liaoxi and Liaodong, where Wuhuan and Mo tribesmen harried them until they were all but wiped out.

sentences.zh: 弇從幸舂陵，因見自請北收上谷兵未發者，定彭寵於漁陽，取張豐於涿郡，還收富平、獲索，東攻張步，以平齊地。

translations.literal: Yan followed attended Chunling, thereby saw self requested north collect Shanggu troops not yet dispatched, settle Peng Chong at Yuyang, take Zhang Feng at Zhuo commandery, return collect Fuping, Huosuo, east attack Zhang Bu, in order to pacify Qi lands.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan accompanied the emperor to Chunling and, on audience, volunteered to march north, gather the Shanggu troops not yet committed, crush Peng Chong at Yuyang, seize Zhang Feng in Zhuo, mop up the Fuping and Huosuo bands, then strike east at Zhang Bu to bring the Qi region to heel.

sentences.zh: 帝壯其意，乃許之。

translations.literal: Emperor deemed his intention stalwart, thereupon permitted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor admired his resolve and granted the request.

sentences.zh: 四年，詔弇進攻漁陽。

translations.literal: Fourth year, ordered Yan advance attack Yuyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of the reign he was ordered to advance on Yuyang.

sentences.zh: 弇以父據上谷，本與彭寵同功，又兄弟無在京師者，自疑，不敢獨進，上書求詣洛陽。

translations.literal: Yan because father occupied Shanggu, originally with Peng Chong same merit, moreover brothers none at capital within, self doubted, not dared alone advance, submitted memorial sought go to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Because his father held Shanggu and had once stood on equal footing with Peng Chong, and because none of his brothers were at court as hostages, Geng Yan hesitated to advance alone; he memorialized asking permission to come to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 詔報曰：『將軍出身舉宗為國，所向陷敵，功效尤著，何嫌何疑，而欲求征？

translations.literal: Edict replied saying: 'General from body raised whole clan for state, wherever faced sank enemy, merit effect especially manifest, what suspicion what doubt, and desire seek summons?
translations.idiomatic: The edict answered: 'You have brought your whole clan into the state's service; wherever you have turned you have broken the enemy, and your achievements stand foremost. What cause have you for mistrust or doubt that you should ask to be recalled?'

sentences.zh: 且與王常共屯涿郡，勉思方略。

translations.literal: Moreover with Wang Chang together garrison Zhuo commandery, strive think strategy plan.
translations.idiomatic: For now camp with Wang Chang in Zhuo commandery and give your full mind to strategy.'

sentences.zh: 』況聞弇求征，亦不自安，遣舒弟國入侍。

translations.literal: ' Kuang heard Yan sought summons, also not self at peace, dispatched Shu's younger brother Guo enter attend.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Kuang heard that his son had asked to report to the capital, he too grew uneasy and sent Geng Shu's younger brother Geng Guo to court as a hostage.

sentences.zh: 帝善之，進封況為隃糜侯。

translations.literal: Emperor deemed it good, advanced enfeoff Kuang as Yumi marquis.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved and advanced Geng Kuang to marquis of Yumi.

sentences.zh: 乃命弇與建義大將軍朱祐、漢忠將軍王常等擊望都、故安西山賊十餘營，皆破之。

translations.literal: Thereupon ordered Yan with Establish Righteousness Great General Zhu You, Han Loyalty General Wang Chang and others strike Wangdu, Gu'an west mountain bandits more than ten camps, all defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: He then ordered Geng Yan with Grand General Who Establishes Righteousness Zhu You, Han Loyalty General Wang Chang, and others to attack the mountain bandits west of Wangdu and Gu'an—more than ten camps—and they carried every position.

sentences.zh: 時，征虜將軍祭遵屯良鄉，驍騎將軍劉喜屯陽鄉，以拒彭寵。

translations.literal: At that time, Campaign Against the Caitiffs General Ji Zun garrisoned Liangxiang, Valiant Cavalry General Liu Xi garrisoned Yangxiang, in order to resist Peng Chong.
translations.idiomatic: Campaign general Ji Zun held Liangxiang and valiant cavalry general Liu Xi held Yangxiang to block Peng Chong.

sentences.zh: 寵遣弟純將匈奴二千餘騎，寵自引兵數萬，分為兩道以擊遵、喜。

translations.literal: Chong dispatched younger brother Chun led Xiongnu more than two thousand horse, Chong self led troops several ten thousand, divided into two roads in order to strike Zun, Xi.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong sent his brother Peng Chun with over two thousand Xiongnu horsemen while he himself led several tens of thousands in two columns against Ji Zun and Liu Xi.

sentences.zh: 胡騎經軍都，舒襲破其眾，斬匈奴兩王，寵乃退走。

translations.literal: Hu horse passed Jundu, Shu surprise attacked defeated their masses, beheaded Xiongnu two kings, Chong thereupon retreated fled.
translations.idiomatic: As the Hu cavalry passed Jundu, Geng Shu fell on them and broke their host, beheading two Xiongnu kings; Peng Chong then withdrew.

sentences.zh: 況復與舒攻寵，取軍都。

translations.literal: Kuang again with Shu attacked Chong, took Jundu.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kuang joined Geng Shu in another strike at Peng Chong and took Jundu.

sentences.zh: 五年，寵死，天子嘉況功，使光祿大夫持節迎況，賜甲第，奉朝請。

translations.literal: Fifth year, Chong died, Son of Heaven praised Kuang's merit, ordered Imperial Household Grandee hold tally welcome Kuang, bestowed first-ranked mansion, attend court summons.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year Peng Chong died. The emperor commended Geng Kuang's service and sent an imperial household grandee with credentials of authority to escort him to the capital, granted him a mansion of the first rank, and enrolled him among those summoned to court.

sentences.zh: 封舒為牟平侯。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Shu as Mouping marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Shu was enfeoffed as marquis of Mouping.

sentences.zh: 遣弇與吳漢擊富平、獲索賊於平原，大破之，降者四萬餘人。

translations.literal: Dispatched Yan with Wu Han strike Fuping, Huosuo bandits at Pingyuan, greatly defeated them, surrendered more than forty thousand people.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan and Wu Han were sent against the Fuping and Huosuo bandits on the Pingyuan plain; they shattered them and received the surrender of more than forty thousand men.

sentences.zh: 因詔弇進討張步。

translations.literal: Thereby ordered Yan advance punish Zhang Bu.
translations.idiomatic: He then ordered Geng Yan to march against Zhang Bu.

sentences.zh: 弇悉收集降卒，結部曲，置將吏，率騎都尉劉歆、太山太守陳俊引兵而東，從朝陽橋濟河以度。

translations.literal: Yan fully collected surrendered soldiers, organized division companies, set generals and clerks, led Cavalry Commandant Liu Xin, Taishan governor Chen Jun led troops and east, from Chaoyang bridge crossed river to cross.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan incorporated the surrendered troops into units with their own officers, then led Cavalry Commandant Liu Xin and Taishan governor Chen Jun eastward, crossing the river at the Chaoyang bridge.

sentences.zh: 張步聞之，乃使其大將軍費邑軍歷下，又分兵屯祝阿，別於太山鐘城列營數十以待弇。

translations.literal: Zhang Bu heard it, then ordered his great general Fei Yi army Lixia, again divided troops garrison Zhu'e, separately at Taishan Zhongcheng arrayed camps several tens to await Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu responded by posting his grand general Fei Yi at Lixia, detaching troops to Zhu'e, and stringing several dozen camps from Taishan's Zhongcheng to await Geng Yan.

sentences.zh: 弇度河先擊祝阿，自旦攻城，日未中而拔之，故開圍一角，令其眾得奔歸鐘城。

translations.literal: Yan crossed river first struck Zhu'e, from dawn attacked city, sun not yet middle and pulled it, intentionally opened siege one corner, ordered their masses able flee return Zhongcheng.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan crossed the river and struck Zhu'e first, storming the walls from dawn until the city fell before noon. He deliberately left one corner of the ring open so the defenders could flee toward Zhongcheng.

sentences.zh: 鐘城人聞祝阿已潰，大恐懼，遂空壁亡去。

translations.literal: Zhongcheng people heard Zhu'e already collapsed, greatly feared, thereupon emptied walls fled departed.
translations.idiomatic: When the garrison at Zhongcheng heard Zhu'e had fallen, panic emptied the fort and the men ran.

sentences.zh: 費邑分遣弟敢守巨裏。

translations.literal: Fei Yi separately dispatched younger brother Gan guard Julü.
translations.idiomatic: Fei Yi sent his brother Fei Gan to hold Julü.

sentences.zh: 弇進兵先脅巨裏，使多伐樹木，揚言以填塞坑塹。

translations.literal: Yan advanced troops first threatened Julü, ordered many cut trees, loudly said in order to fill block ditches moats.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan advanced on Julü and had his men fell timber in quantity, announcing loudly that they meant to fill in the ditches and ramparts.

sentences.zh: 數日，有降者言邑聞弇欲攻巨裏，謀來救之。

translations.literal: Several days, there was surrenderer who said Yi heard Yan desired attack Julü, planned come save it.
translations.idiomatic: After a few days a deserter reported that Fei Yi, hearing Geng Yan intended to storm Julü, was planning a relief march.

sentences.zh: 弇乃嚴令軍中趣修攻具，宣敕諸部，後三日當悉力攻巨裏城。

translations.literal: Yan then strictly ordered army hurry repair siege tools, proclaimed order various divisions, after three days ought full strength attack Julü city.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan issued strict orders to rush siege equipment and proclaimed to every division that in three days they would throw their full strength against Julü.

sentences.zh: 陰緩生口，令得亡歸。

translations.literal: Secretly relaxed captives, ordered able flee return.
translations.idiomatic: He secretly relaxed his guard on prisoners so they could escape.

sentences.zh: 歸者以弇期告邑，邑至日果自將精兵三萬餘人來救之。

translations.literal: Returners with Yan's deadline told Yi, Yi on the day indeed self led crack troops more than thirty thousand people came save it.
translations.idiomatic: The escapees brought word of Geng Yan's timetable to Fei Yi, who on the appointed day led more than thirty thousand picked troops to the relief.

sentences.zh: 弇喜，謂諸將曰：『吾所以修攻具者，欲誘致邑耳。

translations.literal: Yan pleased, told various generals saying: 'My by-which repair siege tools, desire lure bring Yi only.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan was delighted. 'The reason I paraded those siege engines,' he told his generals, 'was to bait Fei Yi out.'

sentences.zh: 今來，適其所求也。

translations.literal: Now comes, fits that-which sought.
translations.idiomatic: Now that he has come, he has given us exactly what we wanted.'

sentences.zh: 』即分三千人守巨裏，自引精兵上岡阪，乘高合戰，大破之，臨陳斬邑。

translations.literal: ' Immediately divided three thousand men guard Julü, self led crack troops ascend hill slope, riding high joined battle, greatly defeated them, at array beheaded Yi.
translations.idiomatic: He left three thousand men to hold Julü, led his crack force up the ridge, and from the high ground engaged and routed Fei Yi, cutting him down in the melee.

sentences.zh: 既而收首級以示巨裏城中，城中兇懼，費敢悉眾亡歸張步。

translations.literal: Afterward collected heads to show Julü city within, city within fierce feared, Fei Gan whole masses fled return Zhang Bu.
translations.idiomatic: He then had the heads displayed outside Julü; terror seized the garrison, and Fei Gan fled with his entire command to Zhang Bu.

sentences.zh: 弇復收其積聚，縱兵擊諸未下者，平四十餘營，遂定濟南。

translations.literal: Yan again collected their stored accumulations, released troops strike various not yet taken, pacified more than forty camps, thereupon settled Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan seized their stores, unleashed his troops on the remaining strongholds, reduced more than forty camps, and brought Jinan commandery under control.

sentences.zh: 時，張步都劇，使其弟藍將精兵二萬守西安，諸郡太守合萬餘人守臨淄，相去四十里。

translations.literal: At that time, Zhang Bu capital Ju, ordered his younger brother Lan lead crack troops twenty thousand guard Xi'an, various commandery governors together more than ten thousand guard Linzi, mutually apart forty li.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu had his capital at Ju. He sent his brother Zhang Lan with twenty thousand elite troops to hold Xi'an while the commandery governors jointly garrisoned Linzi with over ten thousand men; the two towns stood forty li apart.

sentences.zh: 弇進軍畫中，居二城之間。

translations.literal: Yan advanced army Huazhong, dwelt between two cities.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan advanced to Huazhong and camped between the two cities.

sentences.zh: 弇視西安城小而堅，且藍兵又精，臨淄名雖大而實易攻，乃敕諸校會，後五日攻西安。

translations.literal: Yan viewed Xi'an city small yet firm, moreover Lan's troops again crack, Linzi name although large but in fact easy attack, then ordered various colonels assemble, after five days attack Xi'an.
translations.idiomatic: Xi'an was small but strongly held, and Zhang Lan's troops were picked men; Linzi sounded imposing but would be easier to take. Geng Yan summoned his colonels and announced an assault on Xi'an in five days.

sentences.zh: 藍聞之，晨夜儆守。

translations.literal: Lan heard it, dawn night alert guarded.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Lan heard the news and kept his men on alert day and night.

sentences.zh: 至期夜半，弇敕諸將皆蓐食，會明至臨淄城。

translations.literal: Arrived deadline midnight, Yan ordered various generals all eat in bed, assemble bright reach Linzi city.
translations.idiomatic: When the night of the deadline came, Geng Yan ordered his generals to take breakfast in their armor and be at the walls of Linzi by first light.

sentences.zh: 護軍荀梁等爭之，以為宜速攻西安。

translations.literal: Protector-of-the-army Xun Liang and others disputed it, thought ought speedily attack Xi'an.
translations.idiomatic: The protector of the army, Xun Liang, and others objected, arguing that Xi'an should be taken at once.

sentences.zh: 弇曰：『不然。

translations.literal: Yan said: 'Not so.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan said, 'No.'

sentences.zh: 西安聞吾欲攻之，日夜為備；

translations.literal: Xi'an hearing I desire attack it, day night make preparations;
translations.idiomatic: Xi'an has heard we mean to strike it and stands ready night and day;

sentences.zh: 臨淄出不意而至，必驚擾，吾攻之一日必拔。

translations.literal: Linzi if out-of-expectation arrive, must alarm and disturb, I attack it one day must pull.
translations.idiomatic: Linzi will be thrown into panic if we appear where we are not expected; I can storm it in a single day.'

sentences.zh: 拔臨淄即西安孤，張藍與步隔絕，必復亡去，所謂擊一而得二者也。

translations.literal: Pull Linzi then Xi'an isolated, Zhang Lan with Bu cut off, must again flee depart, so-called strike one and obtain two.
translations.idiomatic: Take Linzi and Xi'an is isolated; Zhang Lan is cut off from Zhang Bu and will flee again—that is what men call one blow for two gains.'

sentences.zh: 若先攻西安，不卒下，頓兵堅城，死傷必多。

translations.literal: If first attack Xi'an, not finish down, halt troops hard city, dead wounded must many.
translations.idiomatic: If we strike Xi'an first and fail to carry it quickly, we will stall before a strong wall and lose many men.'

sentences.zh: 縱能拔之，藍引軍還奔臨淄，並兵合勢，觀人虛實，吾深入敵地，後無轉輸，旬日之間，不戰而困。

translations.literal: Even if able pull it, Lan lead army return flee Linzi, combine troops join power, observe people's void-solid, I deep enter enemy land, rear no transport relay, within ten days, not battle but trapped.
translations.idiomatic: Even if we did take it, Zhang Lan would race back to Linzi, combine his forces, and watch for weakness. We would be deep in hostile country with no supply line; within ten days we would be trapped without a fight.'

sentences.zh: 諸君之言，未見其宜。

translations.literal: Gentlemen's words, not yet seen its fitting.
translations.idiomatic: Your counsel does not convince me.'

sentences.zh: 』遂攻臨淄，半日拔之，入據其城。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon attacked Linzi, half day pulled it, entered occupied that city.
translations.idiomatic: He attacked Linzi and had it in half a day, then marched in and held the city.'

sentences.zh: 張藍聞之大懼，遂將其眾亡歸劇。

translations.literal: Zhang Lan heard it greatly feared, thereupon led his masses fled return Ju.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Lan was terrified and withdrew his entire force to Ju.

sentences.zh: 弇乃令軍中無得妄掠劇下，須張步至乃取之，以激怒步。

translations.literal: Yan then ordered army within not obtain wanton plunder below Ju, must wait Zhang Bu arrive then take it, in order to anger Bu.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan forbade any random pillaging toward Ju, ordering his men to wait until Zhang Bu himself arrived before moving in—deliberately baiting Zhang Bu.

sentences.zh: 步聞大笑曰：『以尤來、大彤十餘萬眾，吾皆即其營而破之。

translations.literal: Bu heard greatly laughed saying: 'With Youlai, Great Shape more than hundred thousand masses, I all at their camps and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu roared with laughter when he heard it. 'I broke the Youlai and Great Shape hosts—well over a hundred thousand—right in their camps.'

sentences.zh: 今大耿兵少於彼，又皆疲勞，何足懼乎！

translations.literal: Now great Geng troops fewer than those, again all weary tired, what sufficient fear!
translations.idiomatic: Great Geng's army is smaller than theirs and worn out besides—why should I fear it?'

sentences.zh: 』乃與三弟藍、弘、壽及故大彤渠帥重異等兵號二十萬，至臨淄大城東，將攻弇。

translations.literal: ' Then with three younger brothers Lan, Hong, Shou and former Great Shape channel chief Zhong Yi and others troops titled two hundred thousand, reached Linzi great city east, will attack Yan.
translations.idiomatic: He marched east of greater Linzi with his brothers Zhang Lan, Zhang Hong, and Zhang Shou, the former Great Shape leader Zhong Yi, and a host said to number two hundred thousand, intent on crushing Geng Yan.

sentences.zh: 弇先出兵淄水上，與重異遇，突騎欲縱，弇恐挫其鋒，令步不敢進，故示弱以盛其氣，乃引歸小城，陳兵於內。

translations.literal: Yan first dispatched troops on Zi River above, met Zhong Yi, sudden-riding desired release, Yan feared blunt their point, ordered Bu not dare advance, therefore showed weakness to swell their qi, then led return small city, arrayed troops within.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan first moved onto the Zi River and met Zhong Yi. He held back his shock cavalry, fearing to blunt the enemy's edge and make Zhang Bu hesitate to advance; feigning weakness he drew back into the lesser city and drew up his lines inside.

sentences.zh: 步氣盛，直攻弇營，與劉歆等合戰，弇升王宮壞臺望之，視歆等鋒交，乃自引精兵以橫突步陳於東城下，大破之。

translations.literal: Bu qi flourishing, straight attacked Yan camp, with Liu Xin and others joined battle, Yan ascended royal palace ruined tower watched them, saw Xin and others point cross, then self led crack troops to horizontally strike Bu array at east city below, greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu, flushed with confidence, drove straight at Geng Yan's camp and closed with Liu Xin. From the ruined tower of the royal palace Geng Yan watched the clash of their vanguards, then led picked troops in a slashing charge against Zhang Bu's formation below the eastern wall and shattered him.

sentences.zh: 飛矢中弇股，以佩刀截之，左右無知者。

translations.literal: Flying arrow hit Yan thigh, used belt knife cut it, left right none knew.
translations.idiomatic: A stray arrow struck Geng Yan in the thigh; he cut the shaft with his belt dagger, and none beside him knew he was wounded.

sentences.zh: 至暮罷。

translations.literal: Reached dusk ceased.
translations.idiomatic: The fighting ended at dusk.

sentences.zh: 弇明旦復勒兵出。

translations.literal: Yan next dawn again urged troops out.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn the next day Geng Yan formed his men again and sortied.

sentences.zh: 是時，帝在魯，聞弇為步所攻，自往救之，未至。

translations.literal: At this time, emperor at Lu, heard Yan by Bu attacked, self went save him, not yet arrived.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was at Lu when he learned that Zhang Bu had Geng Yan under pressure; he set out in person to relieve him but had not yet arrived.

sentences.zh: 陳俊謂弇曰：『劇虜兵盛，可且閉營休士，以須上來。

translations.literal: Chen Jun told Yan saying: 'Ju bandits troops flourishing, can for the nonce close camp rest soldiers, to await superior come.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Jun said to Geng Yan, 'The enemy at Ju is strong; better shut the camp, rest the troops, and wait for His Majesty.'

sentences.zh: 』弇曰：『乘輿且到，臣子當擊牛釃酒以待百官，反欲以賊虜遺君父邪？

translations.literal: ' Yan said: 'Imperial conveyance about arrive, subject-son ought strike ox sprinkle wine to await hundred officials, instead desire with bandit caitiffs leave lord father?
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan replied, 'The imperial train is almost here. A minister should slaughter an ox and pour the wine to welcome the court, not leave bandits for his sovereign to deal with!'

sentences.zh: 』乃出兵大戰，自旦及昏，復大破之，殺傷無數，城中溝塹皆滿。

translations.literal: ' Then dispatched troops great battle, from dawn reach dusk, again greatly defeated them, kill wound without count, city within ditches moats all full.
translations.idiomatic: He threw his army into battle from dawn to dusk, broke the enemy again with countless casualties, and filled the city ditches with the dead.

sentences.zh: 弇知步困將退，豫置左右翼為伏以待之。

translations.literal: Yan knew Bu trapped about retreat, beforehand set left right wings as ambush to await them.
translations.idiomatic: Knowing Zhang Bu was cornered and would pull back, Geng Yan posted ambushes on both flanks in advance.

sentences.zh: 人定時，步果引去，伏兵起縱擊，追至鉅昧水上，八九十里僵屍相屬，收得輜重二千餘兩。

translations.literal: At rending-of-men hour, Bu indeed led depart, ambush troops rose release strike, pursued to Jumei River above, eighty ninety li stiff corpses linked one another, collected baggage trains more than two thousand liang.
translations.idiomatic: At the hour of rest Zhang Bu did withdraw; the ambush rose and struck, and the pursuit ran eighty or ninety li to the Jumei River, corpses strewn the whole way. They captured more than two thousand baggage carts.

sentences.zh: 步還劇，兄弟各分兵散去。

translations.literal: Bu returned Ju, brothers each divided troops scatter depart.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu fled to Ju; his brothers broke up their commands and scattered.

sentences.zh: 後數日，車駕至臨淄自勞軍，群臣大會。

translations.literal: After several days, imperial carriage reached Linzi self comforted army, hundred officials great assembly.
translations.idiomatic: A few days later the emperor reached Linzi in person to review the army, and the whole court gathered.

sentences.zh: 帝謂弇曰：『昔韓信破歷下以開基，今將軍攻祝阿以發跡，此皆齊之西界，功足相方。

translations.literal: Emperor told Yan saying: 'Formerly Han Xin broke Lixia to open foundation, now general attacked Zhu'e to rise trace, these all Qi's west border, merit sufficient mutually match.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said to Geng Yan, 'Han Xin took Lixia to open the realm; you took Zhu'e to begin your rise—both on Qi’s western marches, and the deeds are well matched.'

sentences.zh: 而韓信襲擊已降，將軍獨拔勁敵，其功乃難於信也。

translations.literal: But Han Xin surprise attacked already surrendered, general alone pulled strong enemy, its merit then harder than Xin's.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Han Xin struck men who had already yielded, whereas you alone overcame a stubborn foe—your feat is the harder of the two.'

sentences.zh: 又田橫亨酈生，及田橫降，高帝詔衛尉不聽為仇。

translations.literal: Also Tian Heng boiled Li Sheng, and when Tian Heng surrendered, Gao Emperor ordered Commandant of Guards not listen as enemy.
translations.idiomatic: When Tian Heng executed Li Yiji and later submitted, Gaozu ordered the commandant of the guards not to avenge the grudge.'

sentences.zh: 張步前亦殺伏隆，若步來歸命，吾當詔大司徒釋其怨，又事尤相類也。

translations.literal: Zhang Bu before also killed Fu Long, if Bu come submit life, I ought order Grand Minister of Education release his resentment, again affair especially mutually similar.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu once killed Fu Long; if he now comes to surrender, I shall tell the grand minister of education to set aside the blood feud—another close parallel.'

sentences.zh: 將軍前在南陽建此大策，常以為落落難合，有誌者事竟成也！

translations.literal: General before at Nanyang established this great plan, always thought sparse sparse hard join, have determination person affair finally completes!
translations.idiomatic: You laid this grand design at Nanyang when most thought it too bold to succeed—yet the determined see their work through!'

sentences.zh: 』弇因復追步，步奔平壽，乃肉袒負斧锧於軍門。

translations.literal: ' Yan thereby again pursued Bu, Bu fled to Pingshou, then bared flesh bore axe chopping-block at army gate.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan pressed the pursuit to Pingshou, where Zhang Bu stripped to the waist and carried an axe and executioner's block to the camp gate in token of submission.

sentences.zh: 弇傳步詣行在所，而勒兵入據其城。

translations.literal: Yan transmitted Bu go to traveling-place, while urged troops enter occupy that city.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan sent Zhang Bu on to the emperor's temporary headquarters and marched his troops in to hold the city.

sentences.zh: 樹十二郡旗鼓，令步兵各以郡人詣旗下，眾尚十餘萬，輜重七千餘兩，皆罷遣歸鄉里。

translations.literal: Set up twelve commanderies' drums and banners, ordered Zhang Bu foot soldiers each by commandery people go to banner below, masses still more than hundred thousand, baggage trains seven thousand plus liang, all dismissed sent return villages.
translations.idiomatic: He raised the drums and banners of the twelve commanderies and had Zhang Bu's footsoldiers muster by native commandery beneath them—still over a hundred thousand men and seven thousand baggage carts—then disbanded them all to their homes.

sentences.zh: 弇復引兵至城陽，降五校餘黨，齊地悉平。

translations.literal: Yan again led troops to Chengyang, surrendered Wuxiao remainder faction, Qi lands all pacified.
translations.idiomatic: He moved on to Chengyang, received the surrender of the Wuxiao remnant, and brought the whole of Qi to peace.

sentences.zh: 振旅還京師。

translations.literal: Shook army returned capital.
translations.idiomatic: He brought the army home to the capital.

sentences.zh: 六年，西拒隗囂，屯兵於漆。

translations.literal: Sixth year, west resisted Wei Ao, garrisoned troops at Qi.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year he held the line west against Wei Ao, with troops encamped at Qi.

sentences.zh: 八年，從上隴。

translations.literal: Eighth year, followed ascended Long.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year he accompanied the emperor up onto Long.

sentences.zh: 明年，與中郎將來歙分部徇安定、北地諸營保，皆下之。

translations.literal: Next year, with palace gentleman Lai Xi divided parts pacified Anding, Beidi various camp forts, all took them.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he and the palace gentleman Lai Xi campaigned in separate columns against the fortified camps of Anding and Beidi and reduced them all.

sentences.zh: 弇凡所平郡四十六，屠城三百，未嘗挫折。

translations.literal: Yan in all pacified commanderies forty-six, slaughtered cities three hundred, never had setback.
translations.idiomatic: In all Geng Yan pacified forty-six commanderies and stormed three hundred walled places without ever suffering a reverse.

sentences.zh: 十二年，況疾病，乘輿數自臨幸。

translations.literal: Twelfth year, Kuang illness, imperial conveyance several times self visited.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year Geng Kuang fell ill; the emperor visited him repeatedly in person.

sentences.zh: 復以國弟廣、舉並為中郎將。

translations.literal: Again made Guo's younger brothers Guang, Ju both palace gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: He also appointed Geng Guo's younger brothers Geng Guang and Geng Ju both as palace gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 弇兄弟六人皆垂青紫，省侍醫藥，當代以為榮。

translations.literal: Yan brothers six persons all hung blue purple, attended medicine, generation took as glory.
translations.idiomatic: All six Geng brothers wore the blue and purple ribbons of high rank and waited on their father with medicine—the age counted it the height of honor.

sentences.zh: 及況卒，謚烈侯，少子霸襲況爵。

translations.literal: When Kuang died, posthumous title Fierce Marquis, youngest son Ba inherited Kuang's noble.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Kuang died he received the posthumous title Marquis Lie; his youngest son Geng Ba inherited his noble rank.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增弇戶邑，上大將軍印綬，罷，以列侯奉朝請。

translations.literal: Thirteenth year, increased Yan household fief, submitted great general seals and ribbons, dismissed, as column marquis attend court summons.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year Geng Yan's fief was enlarged, he surrendered the seals of grand general and was stood down, retaining rank as a full marquis entitled to attend at court.

sentences.zh: 每有四方異議，輒召入問籌策。

translations.literal: Whenever had four quarters unusual debate, then summoned enter asked plans.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever there was a disputed question from any quarter, he was called in to advise.

sentences.zh: 年五十六，永平元年卒，謚為湣侯。

translations.literal: Age fifty-six, Yongping first year died, posthumous title Min Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: He died at fifty-six in the first year of Yongping and was posthumously titled Marquis Min.

sentences.zh: 子忠嗣。

translations.literal: Son Zhong succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Geng Zhong inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 忠以騎都尉擊匈奴於天山，有功。

translations.literal: Zhong as cavalry commandant struck Xiongnu at Tianshan, had merit.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Zhong, as cavalry commandant, fought the Xiongnu at the Tianshan range with distinction.

sentences.zh: 忠卒，子馮嗣。

translations.literal: Zhong died, son Feng succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Zhong died, his son Geng Feng succeeded.

sentences.zh: 馮卒，子良嗣，一名無禁。

translations.literal: Feng died, son Liang succeeded, one name Wu Jin.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Feng died, his son Geng Liang succeeded; Liang also bore the name Wujin.

sentences.zh: 延光中，尚安帝妹濮陽長公主，位至侍中。

translations.literal: In Yanguang, married Emperor An's younger sister Princess Chang of Puyang, rank reached palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: During the Yanguang era he married Emperor An's younger sister, the senior princess of Puyang, and rose to palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 良卒，子協嗣。

translations.literal: Liang died, son Xie succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Liang died, his son Geng Xie succeeded.

sentences.zh: 隃麋侯霸卒，子文金嗣。

translations.literal: Yumi Marquis Ba died, son Wenjin succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Marquis Ba of Yumi died, his son Wenjin inherited.

sentences.zh: 文金卒，子喜嗣。

translations.literal: Wenjin died, son Xi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Wenjin died, his son Xi succeeded.

sentences.zh: 喜卒，子顯嗣，為羽林左監。

translations.literal: Xi died, son Xian succeeded, was left superintendent of the Feathered Forest.
translations.idiomatic: When Xi died, his son Xian succeeded and held the post of left superintendent of the Feathered Forest guard.

sentences.zh: 顯卒，子援嗣。

translations.literal: Xian died, son Yuan succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Xian died, his son Yuan succeeded.

sentences.zh: 尚桓帝妹長社公主，為河東太守。

translations.literal: Married Emperor Huan's younger sister Princess Changshe, was Hedong governor.
translations.idiomatic: He married Emperor Huan's younger sister, the princess of Changshe, and served as governor of Hedong.

sentences.zh: 後曹操誅耿氏，唯援孫弘存焉。

translations.literal: Later Cao Cao executed Geng clan, only Yuan's grandson Hong survived.
translations.idiomatic: Later Cao Cao extirpated the Gengs; only Hong, a grandson of Geng Yuan, was left.

sentences.zh: 牟平侯舒卒，子襲嗣。

translations.literal: Mouping Marquis Shu died, son Xi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Marquis Shu of Mouping died, his son Xi inherited.

sentences.zh: 尚顯宗女隆慮公主。

translations.literal: Married Emperor Ming's daughter Princess Longlü.
translations.idiomatic: He married Emperor Ming's daughter, the princess of Longlü.

sentences.zh: 襲卒，子寶嗣。

translations.literal: Xi died, son Bao succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Xi died, his son Bao succeeded.

sentences.zh: 寶女弟為清河孝王妃。

translations.literal: Bao's younger sister was Princess consort of filial Prince of Qinghe.
translations.idiomatic: Bao's younger sister was consort to the filial prince of Qinghe.

sentences.zh: 及安帝立，尊孝王，母為孝德皇后，以妃為甘園大貴人。

translations.literal: When Emperor An was established, honored filial prince, mother as Empress Xiaode, took consort as great noble lady of Gan Garden.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor An came to the throne, the filial prince's mother was honored as Empress Xiaode and the consort became senior noble lady of the Gan Garden.

sentences.zh: 帝以寶元舅之重，使監羽林左騎，位至大將軍。

translations.literal: Emperor because Bao first maternal uncle's weight, made supervise Feathered Forest left cavalry, rank reached great general.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, attaching great weight to Bao as his mother's brother, put him over the left wing of the Feathered Forest guard and raised him to grand general.

sentences.zh: 而附事內寵，與中常侍樊豐、帝乳母王聖等譖廢皇太子為濟陰王，及排陷太尉楊震，議者怨之。

translations.literal: But attached serving inner favorites, with palace attendant Fan Feng, emperor's wet-nurse Wang Sheng and others slandered deposed imperial heir as prince of Jiyin, and framed Grand Commandant Yang Zhen, debaters resented them.
translations.idiomatic: He curried favor with the inner court; with the palace attendant Fan Feng, the emperor's wet nurse Wang Sheng, and others he slandered the heir apparent into demotion as prince of Jiyin and ruined Grand Commandant Yang Zhen—men of judgment detested him for it.

sentences.zh: 寶弟子承襲公主爵為林慮侯，位至侍中。

translations.literal: Bao's younger brother's son Cheng inherited princess noble as marquis of Linlü, rank reached palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: Bao's nephew Cheng inherited the princess's fief as marquis of Linlü and rose to palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 安帝崩，閻太后以寶等阿附嬖倖，共為不道，策免寶及承，皆貶爵為亭侯，遣就國。

translations.literal: Emperor An died, Empress Dowager Yan because Bao and others fawned attaching favorites, together acted not-way, edict dismissed Bao and Cheng, all demoted noble to village marquis, dispatched go fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor An died, Empress Dowager Yan charged Bao and his allies with toadying to favorites and acting lawlessly; by edict she dismissed Bao and Cheng, reduced them both to village marquis rank, and sent them to their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 寶於道自殺，國除。

translations.literal: Bao on road self-killed, state abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Bao killed himself on the way; the marquisate was abolished.

sentences.zh: 大貴人數為耿氏請，陽嘉三年，順帝遂紹封寶子箕牟平侯，為侍中。

translations.literal: Great noble lady several times for Geng clan pleaded, Yangjia third year, Shun Emperor then continued enfeoff Bao's son Ji as Mouping marquis, was palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: The senior noble lady repeatedly interceded for the Gengs; in the third year of Yangjia Emperor Shun restored Bao's son Ji as marquis of Mouping and made him a palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 以恒為陽亭侯，承為羽林中郎將。

translations.literal: Made Heng marquis of Yang village, Cheng as Feathered Forest palace gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Heng was enfeoffed marquis of Yang village and Geng Cheng as palace gentleman of the Feathered Forest guard.

sentences.zh: 其後貴人薨，大將軍梁冀從承求貴人珍玩，不能得，冀怒，風有司奏奪其封。

translations.literal: Afterward noble lady died, great general Liang Ji from Cheng sought noble lady rare curios, not able obtain, Ji angry, wind officials memorialize strip their enfeoffment.
translations.idiomatic: After the noble lady died, Grand General Liang Ji demanded her rare treasures from Cheng; when he was refused, Liang Ji in fury prompted officials to memorialize stripping their titles.

sentences.zh: 承惶恐，遂亡匿於穰。

translations.literal: Cheng fearful, then fled hid at Rang.
translations.idiomatic: Cheng fled in terror and hid at Rang.

sentences.zh: 數年，冀推跡得之，乃並族其家十餘人。

translations.literal: Several years, Ji traced tracks obtained him, then together exterminated his family more than ten persons.
translations.idiomatic: Years later Liang Ji tracked him down and exterminated his household of more than ten.

sentences.zh: 論曰：淮陰延論項王，審料成勢，則知高祖之廟勝矣。

translations.literal: Discussion says: Huaiyin extended discussing Xiang King, examined measured completed situation, then knew Gaozu's temple victory.
translations.idiomatic: The appraisal runs: When the Marquis of Huaiyin discoursed on the Hegemon-King of Chu and weighed how matters stood, one could see that Gaozu had already won in the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 耿弇決策河北，定計南陽，亦見光武之業成矣。

translations.literal: Geng Yan decided plan Hebei, fixed plan Nanyang, also saw Guangwu's enterprise completed.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan laid his plans in Hebei and Nanyang alike, and there too one could see that Guangwu's work was already assured.

sentences.zh: 然弇自克拔全齊，而無復尺寸功。

translations.literal: Yet Yan from self overcame pulled whole Qi, yet no again inch-foot merit.
translations.idiomatic: Yet from the time he conquered all Qi onward, Geng Yan won no further territory of note.

sentences.zh: 夫豈不懷？

translations.literal: Husband how not cherish?
translations.idiomatic: Did he no longer hunger for glory?

sentences.zh: 將時之度數，不足以相容乎？

translations.literal: Or timing's measure number, not sufficient mutually contain?
translations.idiomatic: Or did the times and their measure no longer leave room for him?

sentences.zh: 三世為將，道家所忌，而耿氏累葉以功名自終。

translations.literal: Three generations as general, Daoist house what is taboo, yet Geng clan many generations with merit fame self ended.
translations.idiomatic: Three generations of generals are what Daoist teaching warns against, yet the Gengs passed generation after generation in merit and honor to the end.

sentences.zh: 將其用兵欲以殺止殺乎？

translations.literal: Or their use troops desire by killing stop killing?
translations.idiomatic: Did they wield arms intending to end killing with killing?

sentences.zh: 何其獨能隆也！

translations.literal: How alone able flourish!
translations.idiomatic: How alone could they have flourished so!

sentences.zh: 國字叔慮，建武四年初入侍，光武拜為黃門侍郎，應對左右，帝以為能，遷射聲校尉。

translations.literal: Guo styled Shulü, Jianwu fourth year first entered attend, Guangwu appointed as Yellow Gate gentleman-attendant, answered paired left right, emperor thought capable, promoted She-sheng colonel.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Guo, courtesy name Shulü, first entered attendance in the fourth year of Jianwu. Guangwu appointed him gentleman-attendant at the Yellow Gates; he acquitted himself at the ruler's side and was judged capable, then promoted to colonel of the She-sheng corps.

sentences.zh: 七年，射聲官罷，拜駙馬都尉。

translations.literal: Seventh year, She-sheng office abolished, appointed coach commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year the She-sheng post was abolished and he was named coach commandant.

sentences.zh: 父況卒，國於次當嗣，上疏以先侯愛少子霸，固自陳讓，有詔許焉。

translations.literal: Father Kuang died, Guo in order ought inherit, submitted memorial because former marquis loved youngest son Ba, firmly self stated yield, had edict permitted.
translations.idiomatic: When his father Geng Kuang died, Geng Guo stood next in line to inherit but memorialized that the late marquis had favored his youngest son Ba; he firmly declined in his own person, and an edict granted the request.

sentences.zh: 後歷頓丘、陽翟、上蔡令，所在吏人稱之。

translations.literal: Afterward served as magistrate of Dunqiu, Yangzhai, Shangcai, wherever clerks and people praised him.
translations.idiomatic: He later served as magistrate of Dunqiu, Yangzhai, and Shangcai, winning praise from officials and commoners wherever he went.

sentences.zh: 徵為五官中郎將。

translations.literal: Summoned as five offices palace gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: He was summoned to the post of palace gentleman of the five offices.

sentences.zh: 是時，烏桓、鮮卑屢寇外境，國素有籌策，數言邊事，帝器之。

translations.literal: At this time, Wuhuan, Xianbei repeatedly raided outer borders, Guo always had plans, several times spoke border affairs, emperor esteemed him.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the Wuhuan and Xianbei harried the frontier again and again; Geng Guo was full of stratagems, spoke often on border policy, and the emperor valued him.

sentences.zh: 及匈奴薁日逐王比自立為呼韓邪單元，款塞稱藩，願扞禦北虜。

translations.literal: When Xiongnu Ailizhuyu King Bi self-established as Chanyu Huhanye, opened barrier called vassal, wished defend north caitiffs.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xiongnu prince Bi, the Ailizhuyu king, set himself up as Chanyu in the manner of Huhanye, crossed the passes as a vassal, and offered to shield the north against other barbarians,

sentences.zh: 事下公卿。

translations.literal: Matter sent down to dukes and ministers.
translations.idiomatic: the matter was referred to the high ministers.

sentences.zh: 議者皆以為天下初定，中國空虛，夷狄情偽難知，不可許。

translations.literal: Debaters all thought empire first settled, central plain empty void, barbarians true-false hard know, not permissible grant.
translations.idiomatic: The consensus was that the empire was newly pacified, the heartland still drained, and barbarian intentions impossible to read—the court should not agree.

sentences.zh: 國獨曰：『臣以為宜如孝宣故事受之，令東扞鮮卑，北拒匈奴，率厲四夷，完復邊郡，使塞下無晏開之警，萬世安寧之策也。

translations.literal: Guo alone said: 'Your subject thinks ought like Filial Emperor Xuan former case receive him, order east block Xianbei, north resist Xiongnu, lead encourage four barbarians, restore complete border commanderies, make below passes no late-opening alarm, ten thousand generations peace quiet plan.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Guo alone said, 'I hold we should follow Emperor Xuan's precedent and accept him, set him to block the Xianbei on the east and hold the Xiongnu on the north, rally the four barbarians, restore the lost border commanderies, and keep the passes free of sudden alarms—that is the plan for peace for ages to come.'

sentences.zh: 』帝從其議，遂立比為南單于。

translations.literal: ' Emperor followed his discussion, thereupon established Bi as South Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor adopted his argument and established Bi as southern chanyu.'

sentences.zh: 由是烏桓、鮮卑保塞自守，北虜遠遁，中國少事。

translations.literal: Thereby Wuhuan, Xianbei guarded passes self-defend, north caitiffs far fled, central plain few affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Thereafter the Wuhuan and Xianbei guarded the line for themselves, the northern raiders withdrew far away, and the heartland knew less trouble.

sentences.zh: 二十七年，代馮勤為大司農。

translations.literal: Twenty-seventh year, replaced Feng Qin as grand minister of agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-seventh year he succeeded Feng Qin as grand minister of agriculture.

sentences.zh: 又上言宜置度遼將軍、左右校尉，屯五原以防逃亡。

translations.literal: Again submitted memorial saying ought establish general who crosses the Liao, left and right colonels, garrison Wuyuan to guard against fugitives.
translations.idiomatic: He further memorialized that a general-across-the-Liao and left and right colonels should be posted with a garrison at Wuyuan to check desertions.

sentences.zh: 永平元年卒官。

translations.literal: Yongping first year died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in the first year of Yongping.

sentences.zh: 顯宗追思國言，後遂置度遼將軍、左右校尉，如其議焉。

translations.literal: Emperor Ming afterward recalled Guo's words, later then established general who crosses the Liao, left and right colonels, as his discussion.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming later remembered Geng Guo's counsel and eventually did institute the general-across-the-Liao and the left and right colonels as he had proposed.

sentences.zh: 國二子：秉，夔。

translations.literal: Guo two sons: Bing, Kui.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Guo had two sons: Geng Bing and Geng Kui.

sentences.zh: 國子秉

translations.literal: Guo son Bing
translations.idiomatic: Geng Guo's son Geng Bing

sentences.zh: 秉字伯初，有偉體，腰帶八圍。

translations.literal: Bing styled Bochu, had great body, waist belt eight girths.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing, courtesy name Bochu, was a man of imposing stature—his girdle took eight turns around his waist.

sentences.zh: 博通書記，能說《司馬兵法》，尤好將帥之略。

translations.literal: Broadly penetrated documents and records, able expound Sima fa, especially loved generals' and commanders' strategies.
translations.idiomatic: He was widely read in letters and archives, could lecture on the Sima fa, and had a special fondness for the art of command.

sentences.zh: 以父任為郎，數上言兵事。

translations.literal: By father's privilege became gentleman-attendant, several times submitted memorials speaking military affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Through his father's rank he entered court as a gentleman-attendant and repeatedly memorialized on military policy.

sentences.zh: 常以中國虛費，邊陲不寧，其患專在匈奴。

translations.literal: Always considered central plain empty waste, border not quiet, its trouble exclusively in Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: He held that the interior was drained by useless expense while the frontier never rested, and that the root of the evil lay with the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 以戰去戰，盛王之道。

translations.literal: By war remove war, flourishing king's way.
translations.idiomatic: To end war through war is the way of a true king.

sentences.zh: 顯宗既有志北伐，陰然其言。

translations.literal: Emperor Ming already had intention north campaign, secretly approved his words.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming already intended a northern expedition and quietly endorsed his views.

sentences.zh: 永平中，召詣省闥，問前後所上便宜方略，拜謁者僕射，遂見親幸。

translations.literal: Within Yongping, summoned go inner chambers, asked before and after submitted convenient stratagems, appointed herald grandee, thereupon saw intimate favor.
translations.idiomatic: During the Yongping era he was summoned to the inner palace to discuss the memorials he had submitted on frontier policy; he was appointed superintendent of heralds and became a trusted favorite.

sentences.zh: 每公卿會議，常引秉上殿，訪以邊事，多簡帝心。

translations.literal: Whenever dukes and ministers met in council, always led Bing ascend hall, consulted on border affairs, mostly matched emperor's heart.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever the high ministers met in council, the emperor would have Geng Bing brought up to discuss border questions, and his answers usually matched what the ruler wished to hear.

sentences.zh: 十五年，拜駙馬都尉。

translations.literal: Fifteenth year, appointed coach commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifteenth year he was named coach commandant.

sentences.zh: 十六年，以騎都尉秦彭為副，與奉車都尉竇固等俱伐北匈奴。

translations.literal: Sixteenth year, with cavalry commandant Qin Peng as deputy, with coach commandant Dou Gu and others together campaigned north Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year, with Cavalry Commandant Qin Peng as his deputy, he joined Coach Commandant Dou Gu in a campaign against the northern Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 虜皆奔走，不戰而還。

translations.literal: Caitiffs all ran fled, without battle and returned.
translations.idiomatic: The enemy fled before a blow was struck, and the army marched home.

sentences.zh: 十七年夏，詔秉與固合兵萬四千騎，復出白山擊車師。

translations.literal: Seventeenth year summer, edict ordered Bing with Gu combine troops fourteen thousand horse, again exit White Mountain strike Jushi.
translations.idiomatic: That summer in the seventeenth year an edict ordered Geng Bing and Dou Gu to merge their commands—fourteen thousand horsemen—and strike Jushi again from White Mountain.

sentences.zh: 車師有後王、前王，前王即後王之子，其廷相去五百餘里。

translations.literal: Jushi has rear king, front king, front king is rear king's son, their courts mutually apart more than five hundred li.
translations.idiomatic: Jushi had a rear king and a front king; the front king was the rear king's son, and their courts lay more than five hundred li apart.

sentences.zh: 固以後王道遠，山谷深，士卒寒若，欲攻前王。

translations.literal: Gu because rear king road far, mountains valleys deep, soldiers cold bitter, desired attack front king.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu argued that the rear king's seat was distant, the valleys deep, and the troops would suffer bitter cold, and wished to strike the nearer front king first.

sentences.zh: 秉議先赴後王，以為並力根本，則前王自服。

translations.literal: Bing debated first rush rear king, thought join force root, then front king self submit.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing urged marching on the rear king first: seize the root with united strength, he said, and the front king would yield of his own accord.

sentences.zh: 固計未決。

translations.literal: Gu's plan not yet decided.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu could not settle on a plan.

sentences.zh: 秉奮身而起曰：『請行前。

translations.literal: Bing roused body and rose saying: 'Request go front.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing sprang up: 'Let me take the van.'

sentences.zh: 』乃上馬，引兵北入，眾軍不得已，遂進。

translations.literal: ' Then mounted horse, led troops north enter, masses of army had no choice, thereupon advanced.
translations.idiomatic: He mounted, led his men north, and the whole force had no choice but to follow.

sentences.zh: 並縱兵抄掠，斬首數千級，收馬、牛十餘萬頭。

translations.literal: Together released troops raid plunder, beheaded heads several thousand grades, collected horses, cattle more than hundred thousand head.
translations.idiomatic: They swept forward looting and killing several thousand heads and rounding up more than a hundred thousand head of horses and cattle.

sentences.zh: 後王安得震怖，從數百騎出迎秉。

translations.literal: Rear king Ande shocked afraid, followed several hundred horse exit welcome Bing.
translations.idiomatic: The rear king Ande was terrified and rode out with a few hundred horsemen to welcome Geng Bing.

sentences.zh: 而固司馬蘇安欲全功歸固，即馳謂安得曰：『漢貴將獨有奉車都尉，天子姊婿，爵為通侯，當先降之。

translations.literal: But Gu's major Su An desired whole merit return Gu, then galloped told Ande saying: 'Han noble generals only have coach commandant, Son of Heaven's elder sister's husband, noble rank penetrating marquis, ought first surrender him.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu's major Su An, eager to credit Dou Gu alone, galloped up to Ande and said, 'Among Han's high commanders only the coach commandant—the Son of Heaven's brother-in-law and a full marquis—deserves your first surrender.'

sentences.zh: 』安得乃還，更令其諸將迎秉。

translations.literal: ' Ande then returned, again ordered his various generals welcome Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Ande turned back and ordered his generals to receive Geng Bing instead.

sentences.zh: 秉大怒，被甲上馬，麾其精騎徑造固壁。

translations.literal: Bing greatly angry, donned armor mounted horse, waved his crack cavalry straight go Gu's rampart.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing was furious. He armored, mounted, and led his picked cavalry straight to the camp of Dou Gu.

sentences.zh: 言曰：『車師王降，訖今不至，請往梟其首。

translations.literal: Spoke saying: 'Jushi king surrendered, until now not arrived, request go display his head.
translations.idiomatic: He announced, 'The king of Jushi has surrendered but has not come in; I ask leave to fetch his head.'

sentences.zh: 』固大驚曰：『且止，將敗事！

translations.literal: ' Gu greatly startled said: 'For the nonce stop, general will ruin affair!
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu cried in alarm, 'Wait—you will wreck everything!'

sentences.zh: 』秉厲聲曰：『受降如受敵。

translations.literal: ' Bing stern voice said: 'Receiving surrender like receiving enemy.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing thundered back, 'Accepting a surrender is like meeting an enemy in battle.'

sentences.zh: 』遂馳赴之。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon galloped rushed them.
translations.idiomatic: He spurred off toward Ande.

sentences.zh: 安得惶恐，走出門，脫帽抱馬足降。

translations.literal: Ande fearful, ran out gate, removed cap embraced horse hoof surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Ande panicked, ran out the gate, doffed his cap, and clung to the horse's hoofs in submission.

sentences.zh: 秉將以詣固。

translations.literal: Bing led him go to Gu.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing brought him to Dou Gu.

sentences.zh: 其前王亦歸命，遂定車師而還。

translations.literal: That front king also submitted life, thereupon settled Jushi and returned.
translations.idiomatic: The front king also submitted, and they pacified Jushi before withdrawing.

sentences.zh: 明年秋，肅宗即位，拜秉征西將軍，遣案行涼州邊境，勞賜保塞羌胡，進屯酒泉，救戊己校尉。

translations.literal: Next year autumn, Emperor Zhang ascended throne, appointed Bing Campaign West general, dispatched inspect march Liangzhou border, comfort-reward guard-pass Qiang and Hu, advanced garrison Jiuquan, rescue colonels of the Wu and Ji.
translations.idiomatic: The next autumn, when Emperor Zhang came to the throne, Geng Bing was named general who campaigns west and sent to review the Liangzhou frontier, reward the Qiang and Hu who guarded the passes, advance to garrison Jiuquan, and relieve the Wu and Ji colonels.

sentences.zh: 秉性勇壯而簡易於事，軍行常自被甲在前，休止不結營部，然遠斥候，明要誓，有警，軍陳立成，士卒皆樂為死。

translations.literal: Bing's nature brave stalwart yet simple easy in affairs, army march always self donned armor in front, rest stop not tied camp divisions, yet far scouts, clear sworn oaths, if alarm, army array instantly formed, soldiers all gladly died for him.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing was bold and plain-dealing: on the march he often rode in the van in full armor, and at halts he did not bother with elaborate camp lines, yet he posted distant scouts, gave clear orders under oath, and at the first alarm his formation snapped into place; his men were glad to die for him.

sentences.zh: 永元二年，代桓虞為光祿勛。

translations.literal: Yongyuan second year, replaced Huan Yu as superintendent of the imperial household.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Yongyuan he succeeded Huan Yu as superintendent of the imperial household.

sentences.zh: 明年夏卒，時年五十餘。

translations.literal: Next year summer died, at age more than fifty.
translations.idiomatic: He died the next summer in his fifties.

sentences.zh: 賜以未棺、玉衣，將作大匠穿冢，假鼓吹，五營騎士三百餘人送葬。

translations.literal: Bestowed with unplaned coffin, jade shroud, chief architect dug tomb, lent drums and pipes, five camps horsemen more than three hundred persons escorted burial.
translations.idiomatic: He was granted an unplaned coffin and a jade burial suit; the chief architect dug his tomb, he was lent a band, and more than three hundred horsemen of the five camps escorted the funeral.

sentences.zh: 謚曰恒侯。

translations.literal: Posthumous title said Constant Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: His posthumous title was Marquis Heng.

sentences.zh: 匈奴聞秉卒，舉國號哭，或至梨面流血。

translations.literal: Xiongnu heard Bing died, whole state wailed, some to lacerated face flowing blood.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xiongnu heard that Geng Bing had died, the whole nation mourned; some slashed their faces until the blood ran.

sentences.zh: 長子沖嗣。

translations.literal: Eldest son Chong succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest son Geng Chong inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 及竇憲敗，以秉竇氏黨，國除。

translations.literal: When Dou Xian defeated, because Bing Dou clan faction, state abolished.
translations.idiomatic: After Dou Xian's fall Geng Bing was counted a partisan of the Dou clan, and the marquisate was abolished.

sentences.zh: 沖官至漢陽太守。

translations.literal: Chong's official rank reached Hanyang governor.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chong rose to be governor of Hanyang.

sentences.zh: 曾孫紀，少有美名，辟公府，曹操甚敬異之，稍遷少府。

translations.literal: Great-grandson Ji, in youth had fair name, summoned to duke's offices, Cao Cao greatly respected him as unusual, gradually promoted minister of the lesser treasury.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandson Geng Ji won an early reputation, was summoned to the offices of the dukes, and Cao Cao thought him remarkable, promoting him step by step to minister of the lesser treasury.

sentences.zh: 紀以操將篡漢，建安二十三年，與太醫令吉平、丞相司直韋晃謀起兵誅操，不克，夷三族。

translations.literal: Ji because Cao about usurp Han, Jian'an twenty-third year, with grand physician-in-chief Ji Ping, chancellor's upright investigator Wei Huang plotted raise troops execute Cao, not succeeded, exterminated three clans.
translations.idiomatic: Believing that Cao Cao was about to seize the Han throne, in the twenty-third year of Jian'an Geng Ji joined Grand Physician-in-Chief Ji Ping and the chancellor's upright investigator Wei Huang in a plot to rise and kill Cao Cao; it failed, and all three clans were extirpated.

sentences.zh: 於時衣冠盛門坐紀罹禍滅者眾矣。

translations.literal: At that time gentry great houses because Ji met disaster extinguished were many.
translations.idiomatic: Many great houses of the gentry were ruined through their connection with Geng Ji.

sentences.zh: 秉弟夔

translations.literal: Bing's younger brother Kui
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kui, younger brother of Geng Bing.

sentences.zh: 夔字定公。

translations.literal: Kui styled Dinggong.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kui's courtesy name was Dinggong.

sentences.zh: 少有氣決。

translations.literal: In youth had spirit and resolution.
translations.idiomatic: Even as a youth he showed courage and resolve.

sentences.zh: 永元初，為車騎將軍竇憲假司馬，北擊匈奴，轉騎都尉。

translations.literal: Yongyuan beginning, was chariots-and-cavalry general Dou Xian acting major, north struck Xiongnu, transferred cavalry commandant.
translations.idiomatic: At the start of Yongyuan he served as acting major to chariots-and-cavalry general Dou Xian in the northern campaign against the Xiongnu, then became a cavalry commandant.

sentences.zh: 三年，憲復出河西，以夔為大將軍左校尉。

translations.literal: Third year, Xian again exited west of the River, made Kui great general's left colonel.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year Dou Xian marched again west of the Yellow River and appointed Geng Kui left colonel to the grand general.

sentences.zh: 將精騎八百，出居延塞，直奔北單于廷，於金微山斬閼氏、名王以下五千餘級，單于與數騎脫亡，盡獲其匈奴珍寶財畜，去塞五千餘里而還，自漢出師所未嘗至也。

translations.literal: Led crack cavalry eight hundred, exited Juyan barrier, straight rushed north Chanyu's court, at Mount Jinyi beheaded queen, famous kings below more than five thousand grades, Chanyu with several horse escaped fled, fully obtained their Xiongnu treasures wealth livestock, departed barrier more than five thousand li and returned, from Han dispatched troops never yet reached.
translations.idiomatic: He led eight hundred picked horsemen out of the Juyan defile and drove straight for the northern chanyu's court. On Mount Jinyi he cut off more than five thousand heads, from the queen and famous kings downward; the chanyu fled with a handful of riders, and Kui seized the full hoard of Xiongnu treasure, wealth, and stock. He returned from more than five thousand li beyond the passes—farther than any Han army had ever marched.

sentences.zh: 乃封夔粟邑侯。

translations.literal: Thereupon enfeoffed Kui as Suyi marquis.
translations.idiomatic: He was thereupon enfeoffed as marquis of Suyi.

sentences.zh: 會北單于弟左鹿蠡王於除自立為單于，眾八部二萬餘人，來居蒲類海上，遣使款塞。

translations.literal: It happened north Chanyu's younger brother left Luli king Yuchu self-established as Chanyu, masses eight divisions more than twenty thousand people, came dwell on Pu Sea, dispatched envoys open barrier.
translations.idiomatic: The northern chanyu's younger brother, the left Luli king Yuchu, set himself up as chanyu with more than twenty thousand men of eight divisions and settled on the Pu Sea, then sent envoys to the passes offering submission.
