sentences.zh: 以夔為中郎將，持節衛護之。

translations.literal: Made Kui palace gentleman, held tally guard-protect him.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kui was named a palace gentleman and sent with credentials to protect him.

sentences.zh: 及竇憲敗，夔亦免官奪爵士。

translations.literal: When Dou Xian defeated, Kui also dismissed office stripped noble rank.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou Xian fell, Geng Kui was likewise dismissed from office and stripped of his title.

sentences.zh: 後復為長水校尉，拜五原太守，遷遼東太守。

translations.literal: Afterward again was colonel of the Long River, appointed Wuyuan governor, transferred Liaodong governor.
translations.idiomatic: Later he served again as colonel of the Long River, was appointed governor of Wuyuan, and was transferred to Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 元興元年，貊人寇郡界，夔追擊，斬其渠帥。

translations.literal: Yuanxing first year, Mo people raided commandery border, Kui pursued struck, beheaded their channel chief.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Yuanxing Mo tribesmen raided the commandery; Geng Kui pursued them and beheaded their leader.

sentences.zh: 永初三年，南單于檀反畔，使夔率鮮卑及諸郡兵屯雁門，與車騎將軍何熙共擊之。

translations.literal: Yongchu third year, south Chanyu Tan rebelled, ordered Kui lead Xianbei and various commanderies troops garrison Yanmen, with chariots-and-cavalry general He Xi together struck them.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Yongchu the southern chanyu Tan rebelled; Geng Kui was ordered to lead the Xianbei and troops of several commanderies to hold Yanmen, joining chariots-and-cavalry general He Xi in the attack.

sentences.zh: 熙推夔為先鋒，而遣其司馬耿溥、劉祉將二千人與夔俱進。

translations.literal: Xi pushed Kui as vanguard, while dispatched his major Geng Pu, Liu Zhi lead two thousand men with Kui together advance.
translations.idiomatic: He Xi named Geng Kui vanguard while sending the majors Geng Pu and Liu Zhi with two thousand men to advance with him.

sentences.zh: 到屬國故城，單于遣薁日逐王三千餘人遮漢兵。

translations.literal: Reached dependent state's old city, Chanyu dispatched Ailizhuyu king more than three thousand men block Han troops.
translations.idiomatic: At the old city of the dependent state the chanyu sent the Ailizhuyu king with more than three thousand men to bar the Han army.

sentences.zh: 夔自擊其左，令鮮卑攻其右，虜遂敗走，追斬千餘級，殺其名王六人，獲穹廬車重千餘兩，馬畜生口甚眾。

translations.literal: Kui self struck their left, ordered Xianbei attack their right, caitiffs thereupon defeated fled, pursued beheaded more than thousand grades, killed their famous kings six men, obtained dome tents baggage trains more than thousand liang, horses livestock captives very many.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kui hit their left while ordering the Xianbei to strike their right; the enemy broke and fled. The pursuit took more than a thousand heads and six of their famous kings, along with over a thousand baggage carts, dome tents, mounts, livestock, and captives without number.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑馬多羸病，遂畔出塞。

translations.literal: Xianbei horses many emaciated sick, thereupon rebelled exited barrier.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbei horses were mostly spent and sick; they mutinied and left the frontier.

sentences.zh: 夔不能獨進，以不窮追，左轉雲中太守，後遷行度遼將軍事。

translations.literal: Kui could not alone advance, because not exhaust pursued, left transferred Yunzhong governor, afterward transferred acting general-across-the-Liao affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Unable to press on alone and faulted for not pursuing to the end, Geng Kui was shifted left to governor of Yunzhong, then promoted to acting general-across-the-Liao.

sentences.zh: 夔勇而有氣，數侵陵使匈奴中郎將鄭戩。

translations.literal: Kui brave yet had spirit, several times encroached bullied palace gentleman to the Xiongnu Zheng Jian.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kui was aggressive by nature and repeatedly browbeat Zheng Jian, the palace gentleman sent to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 元初元年，坐徵下獄，以減死論，笞二百。

translations.literal: Yuanchu first year, sat crime summoned down prison, by reduced death judged, flogged two hundred.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Yuanchu he was charged, thrown into prison, sentenced to commute death, and flogged two hundred strokes.

sentences.zh: 建光中，復拜度遼將軍。

translations.literal: Within Jianguang, again appointed general-across-the-Liao.
translations.idiomatic: During the Jianguang era he was again named general-across-the-Liao.

sentences.zh: 時，鮮卑攻殺雲中太守成嚴，圍烏桓校尉徐常於馬城。

translations.literal: At that time, Xianbei attacked killed Yunzhong governor Cheng Yan, besieged Wuhuan colonel Xu Chang at Ma city.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the Xianbei killed Cheng Yan, governor of Yunzhong, and besieged Wuhuan colonel Xu Chang at Ma city.

sentences.zh: 夔與幽州刺史龐參救之，追虜出塞而還。

translations.literal: Kui with Youzhou governor Pang Can rescued them, pursued caitiffs exited barrier and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kui and Youzhou governor Pang Can relieved them and chased the enemy beyond the passes before turning back.

sentences.zh: 後坐法免，卒於家。

translations.literal: Afterward sat law dismissed, died at home.
translations.idiomatic: Later he was dismissed for an offense and died at home.

sentences.zh: 從子恭

translations.literal: Brother's son Gong
translations.idiomatic: The biography turns to his brother's son, Geng Gong.

sentences.zh: 恭字伯宗，國弟廣之子也。

translations.literal: Gong styled Bozong, was Guo's younger brother Guang's son.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong, courtesy name Bozong, was the son of Geng Guo's younger brother Geng Guang.

sentences.zh: 少孤。

translations.literal: In youth orphaned.
translations.idiomatic: He was orphaned while young.

sentences.zh: 慷慨多大略，有將帥才。

translations.literal: Stirred generous had great plans, had generals' and commanders' talent.
translations.idiomatic: He was high-minded and far-sighted, with a born commander's gifts.

sentences.zh: 永平十七年冬，騎都尉劉張出擊車師，請恭為司馬，與奉車都尉竇固及從弟駙馬都尉秉破降之。

translations.literal: Yongping seventeenth year winter, cavalry commandant Liu Zhang exited strike Jushi, requested Gong as major, with coach commandant Dou Gu and cousin coach commandant Bing broke and surrendered them.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the seventeenth year of Yongping, when Cavalry Commandant Liu Zhang marched against Jushi, he asked for Geng Gong as his major; with Coach Commandant Dou Gu and his cousin Coach Commandant Geng Bing they crushed and received the surrender of Jushi.

sentences.zh: 始置西域都護、戊己校尉，乃以恭為戊己校尉，屯後王部金蒲城，謁者關寵為戊己校尉，屯前王柳中城，屯各置數百人。

translations.literal: First established Western Regions protector, Wu and Ji colonels, then made Gong Wu and Ji colonel, garrisoned rear king's division at Jinfu city, herald Guan Chong as Wu and Ji colonel, garrisoned front king's Liuzhong city, garrisons each placed several hundred men.
translations.idiomatic: When the protector-general of the Western Regions and the Wu and Ji colonels were first instituted, Geng Gong was named colonel of Wu and Ji and stationed with the rear king's people at Jinfu, while the herald Guan Chong was named colonel of Wu and Ji and stationed at Liuzhong with the front king; each garrison held several hundred men.

sentences.zh: 恭至部，移檄烏孫，示漢威德，大昆彌以下皆歡喜，遣使獻名馬，及奉宣帝時所賜公主博具，願遣子入侍。

translations.literal: Gong reached division, transmitted proclamation Wusun, displayed Han might and virtue, great Kunmi down all rejoiced, dispatched envoys presented famous horses, and offered Emperor Xuan-time bestowed princess board games, wished send son enter attend.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Gong reached his command he sent a manifesto to Wusun proclaiming Han might and virtue; the great Kunmi and his court rejoiced, sent envoys with famous horses, returned the board games given the princess under Emperor Xuan, and asked to send a son to court.

sentences.zh: 恭乃發使賫金帛，迎其侍子。

translations.literal: Gong then dispatched envoys carrying gold silks, welcome his attendant son.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong then sent envoys with gold and silks to escort the hostage prince.

sentences.zh: 明年三月，北單于遣左鹿蠡王二萬騎擊車師。

translations.literal: Next year third month, north Chanyu dispatched left Luli king twenty thousand horse strike Jushi.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month of the next year the northern chanyu sent the left Luli king with twenty thousand horsemen against Jushi.

sentences.zh: 恭遣司馬將兵三百人救之，道逢匈奴騎多，皆為所歿。

translations.literal: Gong dispatched major led troops three hundred men save them, road met Xiongnu horse many, all by them destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong sent a major with three hundred men to the relief, but they met overwhelming Xiongnu cavalry on the road and were wiped out.

sentences.zh: 匈奴遂破殺後王安得，而攻金蒲城。

translations.literal: Xiongnu thereupon broke killed rear king Ande, and attacked Jinfu city.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu then broke the rear king Ande and killed him, and turned on Jinfu.

sentences.zh: 恭乘城搏戰，以毒藥傅矢。

translations.literal: Gong mounted city fought hand-to-hand, with poison drug smeared arrows.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong fought from the walls and smeared his arrows with poison.

sentences.zh: 傳語匈奴曰：『漢家箭神，其中瘡者必有異。

translations.literal: Transmitted words Xiongnu saying: 'Han house arrows divine, those within wounded must have strange.
translations.idiomatic: He sent word to the Xiongnu: 'Han arrows are spirit-touched; whoever is struck will suffer wonders.'

sentences.zh: 』因發強弩射之。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon released strong crossbows shot them.
translations.idiomatic: He then loosed his heavy crossbows at them.

sentences.zh: 虜中矢者，視創皆沸，遂大驚。

translations.literal: Caitiffs within arrowed, viewed wounds all boiled, thereupon greatly startled.
translations.idiomatic: Those who were hit found their wounds boiling and were terrified.

sentences.zh: 會天暴風雨，隨雨擊之，殺傷甚眾。

translations.literal: It happened heaven violent wind rain, following rain struck them, kill wound very many.
translations.idiomatic: A violent storm broke; riding the rain his men struck again and inflicted heavy casualties.

sentences.zh: 匈奴震怖，相謂曰：『漢兵神，真可畏也！

translations.literal: Xiongnu shocked afraid, mutually said: 'Han soldiers divine, truly can be feared!
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu were shaken and said to one another, 'The Han soldiers are divine—truly to be feared!'

sentences.zh: 』遂解去。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon unbound departed.
translations.idiomatic: They broke off the siege and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 恭以疏勒城傍有澗水可固，五月，乃引兵據之。

translations.literal: Gong because Shule city beside had stream water could firm, fifth month, then led troops occupied it.
translations.idiomatic: Thinking the stream beside Shule city a defensible position, in the fifth month he withdrew his force there.

sentences.zh: 七月，匈奴復來攻恭，恭募先登數千人直馳之，胡騎散走，匈奴遂於城下擁絕澗水。

translations.literal: Seventh month, Xiongnu again came attack Gong, Gong recruited first ascend several thousand men straight gallop them, Hu horse scattered fled, Xiongnu thereupon at city below dammed cut stream water.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the Xiongnu attacked again; Geng Gong enlisted several thousand volunteers for the escalade and charged them so that the Hu horsemen scattered. The Xiongnu then dammed the stream below the city.

sentences.zh: 恭於城中穿井十五丈不得水，吏士渴乏，笮馬糞汁而飲之。

translations.literal: Gong within city dug well fifteen zhang not obtain water, clerks and soldiers thirsty exhausted, pressed horse dung juice and drank it.
translations.idiomatic: Inside the city he sank a well fifteen zhang deep without finding water; his men were parched and squeezed liquid from horse dung to drink.

sentences.zh: 恭仰嘆曰：『聞昔貳師將軍拔佩刀剌山，飛泉湧出；

translations.literal: Gong looked up sighed saying: 'Heard formerly Second Division general drew belt knife pricked mountain, flying spring gushed out;
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong looked up and sighed, 'They say the Second Division general once drew his sword and stabbed the rock until a spring burst forth;

sentences.zh: 今漢德神明，豈有窮哉。

translations.literal: Now Han virtue spirit bright, how can have exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: How could Han virtue, bright as the spirits, ever be exhausted.

sentences.zh: 』乃整衣服向井再拜，為吏士禱。

translations.literal: ' Then straightened clothes toward well twice bowed, for clerks and soldiers prayed.
translations.idiomatic: He straightened his robes, bowed twice to the well, and prayed for his men.

sentences.zh: 有頃，水泉奔出，眾皆稱萬歲。

translations.literal: After a while, water spring gushed out, masses all called ten thousand years.
translations.idiomatic: Presently water welled up; the soldiers shouted long life to the throne.

sentences.zh: 乃令吏士揚水以示虜。

translations.literal: Then ordered clerks and soldiers raise water to show caitiffs.
translations.idiomatic: He had his men hoist water for the enemy to see.

sentences.zh: 虜出不意，以為神明，遂引去。

translations.literal: Caitiffs out-of-expectation, thought spirit bright, thereupon led depart.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu, taken by surprise, took it for a miracle and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 時，焉耆、龜茲攻歿都護陳睦，北虜亦圍關寵於柳中。

translations.literal: At that time, Yanqi, Qiuci attacked destroyed protector Chen Mu, north caitiffs also besieged Guan Chong at Liuzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Yanqi and Qiuci had destroyed Protector-General Chen Mu, and the northern tribesmen also had Guan Chong under siege at Liuzhong.

sentences.zh: 會顯宗崩，救兵不至，車師復畔，與匈奴共攻恭。

translations.literal: It happened Emperor Ming died, relief troops not arrived, Jushi again rebelled, with Xiongnu together attacked Gong.
translations.idiomatic: Then Emperor Ming died and no relief came; Jushi rebelled again and joined the Xiongnu in attacking Geng Gong.

sentences.zh: 恭歷士眾擊走之。

translations.literal: Gong exhausted officers masses struck routed them.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong rallied his officers and men and drove them off.

sentences.zh: 後王夫人先世漢人，常私以虜情告恭，又給以糧餉。

translations.literal: Rear king's consort former generations Han people, always privately with caitiff situation told Gong, again supplied grain provisions.
translations.idiomatic: The rear king's queen was of Han descent on her mother's side; she secretly reported enemy movements to Geng Gong and supplied him with grain.

sentences.zh: 數月，食盡窮困，乃煮鎧弩，食其筋革。

translations.literal: Several months, food exhausted poor trapped, then boiled armor crossbows, ate their sinew leather.
translations.idiomatic: After months their food was gone; they boiled armor and crossbows and ate the sinew and hide.

sentences.zh: 恭與士推誠同死生，故皆無二心，而稍稍死亡，餘數十人。

translations.literal: Gong with officers pushed sincerity same death life, therefore all had no two hearts, yet gradually gradually died, remainder several tens persons.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong dealt openly with his officers and shared life and death with them, so none harbored a second thought, yet they died off one by one until only a few dozen were left.

sentences.zh: 單于知恭已困，欲必降之。

translations.literal: Chanyu knew Gong already trapped, desire must make him surrender.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu saw that Geng Gong was at his last gasp and was determined to force his surrender.

sentences.zh: 復遣使招恭曰：『若降者，當封為白屋王，妻以女子。

translations.literal: Again dispatched envoy summon Gong saying: 'If surrender, will enfeoff as White House king, wife with woman child.
translations.idiomatic: He sent another envoy with an offer: 'Yield and you shall be king of the white yurt and wed to an imperial daughter.'

sentences.zh: 』恭乃誘其使上城，手擊殺之，炙諸城上。

translations.literal: ' Gong then lured his envoy ascend city, hand struck killed him, roasted on city wall.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong lured the envoy onto the walls, killed him with his own hand, and had the corpse roasted on the parapet.

sentences.zh: 虜官屬望見，號哭而去。

translations.literal: Caitiff officials followers saw from afar, wailed cried and departed.
translations.idiomatic: The enemy officers looked on from afar, broke into lamentation, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 單于大怒，更益兵圍恭，不能下。

translations.literal: Chanyu greatly angry, again increased troops besiege Gong, not able take.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu flew into a rage, threw in more troops to besiege Geng Gong, and still could not storm the city.

sentences.zh: 會關寵已歿，蒙等聞之，便欲引兵還。

translations.literal: It happened Guan Chong already perished, Meng and others heard it, then desired lead troops return.
translations.idiomatic: By then Guan Chong was dead; Wang Meng and his colleagues, hearing the news, prepared to march home.

sentences.zh: 先是，恭遣軍吏范羌至敦煌迎兵士寒服，羌因隨王蒙軍俱出塞。

translations.literal: Before this, Gong dispatched army clerk Fan Qiang to Dunhuang greet soldiers cold clothing, Qiang therefore followed Wang Meng army together exited barrier.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier Geng Gong had sent his army clerk Fan Qiang to Dunhuang for winter clothing for the troops; Fan Qiang had therefore accompanied Wang Meng's army beyond the passes.

sentences.zh: 羌固請迎恭，諸將不敢前，乃分兵二千人與羌，從山北迎恭，遇大雪丈餘，軍僅能至。

translations.literal: Qiang firmly requested welcome Gong, various generals not dare advance, then divided troops two thousand men give Qiang, from mountain north welcome Gong, met great snow more than one zhang, army barely able arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Qiang begged to rescue Geng Gong, but the generals dared not advance until they detached two thousand men to him; he came round the northern slopes through snow more than a fathom deep, and his column barely reached the city.

sentences.zh: 城中夜聞兵馬聲，以為虜來，大驚。

translations.literal: City within night heard horse soldier sounds, thought caitiffs come, greatly startled.
translations.idiomatic: In the night the garrison heard the tramp of horses and thought the enemy had come; panic seized them.

sentences.zh: 羌乃遙呼曰：『我范羌也。

translations.literal: Qiang then distant shouted saying: 'I Fan Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: From the darkness Fan Qiang shouted, 'It is I, Fan Qiang.'

sentences.zh: 漢遣軍迎校尉耳。

translations.literal: Han dispatched army welcome colonel ear.
translations.idiomatic: The court has sent troops to fetch the colonel.'

sentences.zh: 』城中皆稱萬歲。

translations.literal: ' City within all called ten thousand years.
translations.idiomatic: From the walls came a shout of long life to the throne.

sentences.zh: 開門，共相持涕泣。

translations.literal: Opened gate, together mutually held wept cried.
translations.idiomatic: They opened the gates and fell weeping into one another's arms.

sentences.zh: 明日，遂相隨俱歸。

translations.literal: Next day, thereupon mutual followed together returned.
translations.idiomatic: The next day they set out together for home.

sentences.zh: 虜兵追之，且戰且行。

translations.literal: Caitiff troops pursued them, while battled while marched.
translations.idiomatic: Enemy cavalry harried them as they fought their way along.

sentences.zh: 吏士素饑困，發疏勒時尚有二十六人，隨路死沒，三月至玉門，唯餘十三人。

translations.literal: Clerks and soldiers long hungry trapped, when set out Shule still had twenty-six persons, along road died perished, three months reached Jade Gate, only remainder thirteen persons.
translations.idiomatic: The men had long been starving and desperate: twenty-six had marched out of Shule, but they died along the road, and three months later only thirteen reached the Jade Gate.

sentences.zh: 衣屨穿決，形容枯槁。

translations.literal: Clothes shoes pierced torn, appearance form withered dry.
translations.idiomatic: Their clothes and shoes were in rags; their faces were the color of dust.

sentences.zh: 中郎將鄭眾為恭已下洗沭易衣冠。

translations.literal: Palace gentleman Zheng Zhong for Gong and below washed bathed changed clothes caps.
translations.idiomatic: Palace gentleman Zheng Zhong had Geng Gong and his men bathed, given fresh clothes, and reclothed.

sentences.zh: 上疏曰：『耿恭以單兵固守孤城，當匈奴之衝，對數萬之眾，連月逾年，心力困盡。

translations.literal: Submitted memorial saying: 'Geng Gong with single troops firmly guarded lone city, faced Xiongnu's thrust, opposed several ten thousand masses, linked months exceeded year, heart strength exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: He memorialized: 'With a handful of men Geng Gong held a lone city athwart the Xiongnu, facing tens of thousands for month after month until body and will were spent.'

sentences.zh: 鑿山為井，煮弩為糧，出於萬死無一生之望。

translations.literal: Chiseled mountain for well, boiled crossbows for grain, exited from ten thousand deaths no one life hope.
translations.idiomatic: They sank wells through rock, boiled crossbows for food, and marched out of ten thousand deaths where no life seemed possible.'

sentences.zh: 前後殺傷醜虜數千百計，卒全忠勇，不為大漢恥。

translations.literal: Before after kill wound ugly caitiffs several thousand hundred count, finally preserved loyalty courage, not for Great Han shame.
translations.idiomatic: They killed and wounded the enemy by thousands on thousands yet kept faith and valor and brought no shame on the Han.'

sentences.zh: 恭之節義，古今未有。

translations.literal: Gong's integrity righteousness, ancient modern never had.
translations.idiomatic: Such steadfastness is without parallel in any age.'

sentences.zh: 宜蒙顯爵，以厲將帥。

translations.literal: Ought receive manifest noble rank, in order to encourage generals commanders.
translations.idiomatic: He deserves a conspicuous title to hearten every commander.'

sentences.zh: 』及恭至洛陽，鮑昱奏恭節過蘇武，宜蒙爵賞。

translations.literal: ' When Gong reached Luoyang, Bao Yu memorialized Gong's integrity surpassed Su Wu, ought receive noble reward.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Gong reached Luoyang, Bao Yu reported that his steadfastness outdid even Su Wu's and that he should be richly enfeoffed.

sentences.zh: 於是拜為騎都尉，以恭司馬石修為洛陽市丞，張封為雍營司馬，軍吏范羌為共丞，餘九人皆補羽林。

translations.literal: Thereupon appointed cavalry commandant, made Gong's major Shi Xiu Luoyang market assistant, Zhang Feng Yong camp major, army clerk Fan Qiang Gong county assistant, remaining nine persons all supplemented Feathered Forest.
translations.idiomatic: He was named cavalry commandant; Geng Gong's major Shi Xiu became assistant of the Luoyang market, Zhang Feng major of the Yong camp, the army clerk Fan Qiang assistant of Gong county, and the other nine were all enrolled in the Feathered Forest guard.

sentences.zh: 恭母先卒，及還，追行喪制，有詔使五官中郎將賫牛、酒釋服。

translations.literal: Gong's mother before died, when returned, pursued perform mourning regulations, had edict ordered five offices palace gentleman carry ox wine release mourning clothes.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong's mother had died in his absence; on his return he observed mourning until an edict sent a palace gentleman of the five offices with oxen and wine to release him from sackcloth.

sentences.zh: 明年，遷長水校尉。

translations.literal: Next year, transferred colonel of the Long River.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he was transferred to colonel of the Long River.

sentences.zh: 其秋，金城、隴西羌反。

translations.literal: That autumn, Jincheng, Longxi Qiang rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn the Qiang of Jincheng and Longxi rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 恭上疏言方略，詔召入問狀。

translations.literal: Gong submitted memorial spoke stratagem, edict summoned enter asked situation.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong memorialized his strategy and was summoned to give account in person.

sentences.zh: 乃遣恭將五校士三千人，副車騎將軍馬防討西羌。

translations.literal: Then dispatched Gong lead five camps soldiers three thousand men, deputy chariots-and-cavalry general Ma Fang punish west Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: He was then sent with three thousand men of the five camps as deputy to chariots-and-cavalry general Ma Fang against the western Qiang.

sentences.zh: 恭屯枹罕，數與羌接戰。

translations.literal: Gong garrisoned Fuhan, several times with Qiang joined battle.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gong held Fuhan and skirmished repeatedly with the Qiang.

sentences.zh: 明年秋，燒當羌降，防還京師，恭留擊諸未服者，首虜千餘人，獲牛、羊四萬餘頭，勒姐、燒何羌等十三種數萬人，皆詣恭降。

translations.literal: Next year autumn, Shao-dang Qiang surrendered, Fang returned capital, Gong remained strike various not submitted, heads captives more than thousand, obtained cattle sheep more than forty thousand head, Lejie, Shao-he Qiang and others thirteen kinds several ten thousand people, all went to Gong surrender.
translations.idiomatic: The next autumn the Shao-dang Qiang surrendered; Ma Fang went back to the capital while Geng Gong stayed to reduce those still holding out—more than a thousand heads and prisoners, forty thousand-odd head of cattle and sheep, and tens of thousands of the Lejie, Shao-he, and thirteen other Qiang bands, all of whom came to Geng Gong to yield.

sentences.zh: 初，恭出隴西，上言：『故安豐侯竇融昔在西州，甚得羌胡腹心。

translations.literal: At first, Gong exited Longxi, submitted memorial saying: 'Former Marquis of Anfeng Dou Rong once at west region, very obtained Qiang Hu belly heart.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, marching out of Longxi, Geng Gong had written: 'The late Marquis of Anfeng, Dou Rong, once held the west and won the complete trust of Qiang and Hu alike.'

sentences.zh: 今大鴻臚固，即其子孫。

translations.literal: Now grand herald Gu, is his descendant offspring.
translations.idiomatic: The grand herald Dou Gu is his descendant.'

sentences.zh: 前擊白山，功冠三軍。

translations.literal: Before struck White Mountain, merit crowned three armies.
translations.idiomatic: In the White Mountain campaign his merit topped the whole host.'

sentences.zh: 宜奉大使，鎮撫涼部。

translations.literal: Ought receive great envoy, pacify soothe Liang region.
translations.idiomatic: Let him go as a high commissioner to pacify the Liang region.'

sentences.zh: 令車騎將軍防屯軍漢陽，以為威重。

translations.literal: Order chariots-and-cavalry general Fang garrison army Hanyang, to serve as awe weight.
translations.idiomatic: Station chariots-and-cavalry general Ma Fang at Hanyang to lend the weight of his presence.'

sentences.zh: 』由是大忤於防。

translations.literal: ' Thereby greatly offended Fang.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial gravely offended Ma Fang.

sentences.zh: 及防還，監營謁者李譚承旨奏恭不憂軍事，被詔怨望。

translations.literal: When Fang returned, supervising camp herald Li Tan received purpose memorialize Gong not worried military affairs, received edict resentment look.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Fang came back, the camp-supervising herald Li Tan, acting on a hint, memorialized that Geng Gong neglected his command and nursed grievances against the edict.

sentences.zh: 坐徵下獄，免官歸本郡，卒於家。

translations.literal: Sat crime summoned down prison, dismissed office returned original commandery, died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He was charged, cast into prison, stripped of office, and sent home to his commandery, where he died.

sentences.zh: 子溥，為京兆虎牙都尉。

translations.literal: Son Pu, was capital region tiger-fang colonel.
translations.idiomatic: His son Geng Pu served as tiger-fang colonel of the capital region.

sentences.zh: 元初二年，擊畔羌於丁奚城，軍敗，遂歿。

translations.literal: Yuanchu second year, struck rebel Qiang at Dingxi city, army defeated, thereupon perished.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Yuanchu he fought rebel Qiang at Dingxi; his force was broken and he fell in battle.

sentences.zh: 詔拜溥子宏、曄並為郎。

translations.literal: Edict appointed Pu's sons Hong, Ye both gentlemen-attendants.
translations.idiomatic: An edict named both of Pu's sons, Hong and Ye, gentlemen-attendant.

sentences.zh: 恭孫曄

translations.literal: Gong grandson Ye
translations.idiomatic: Geng Ye, grandson of Geng Gong.

sentences.zh: 曄字季遇。

translations.literal: Ye styled Jiyu.
translations.idiomatic: His courtesy name was Jiyu.

sentences.zh: 順帝初，為烏桓校尉。

translations.literal: Emperor Shun beginning, was Wuhuan colonel.
translations.idiomatic: At the beginning of Emperor Shun's reign he was appointed Wuhuan colonel.

sentences.zh: 時，鮮卑寇緣邊，殺代郡太守。

translations.literal: At that time, Xianbei raided border fringe, killed Dai commandery governor.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the Xianbei raided the frontier and killed the governor of Dai.

sentences.zh: 曄率烏桓及諸郡卒出塞討擊，大破之。

translations.literal: Ye led Wuhuan and various commanderies soldiers exited barrier punish strike, greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Ye led the Wuhuan and troops of several commanderies beyond the passes and routed them.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑震怖，數萬人詣遼東降。

translations.literal: Xianbei shocked afraid, several ten thousand people went Liaodong surrender.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbei were terrified; tens of thousands came to Liaodong to surrender.

sentences.zh: 自後頻出輒克獲，威振北方。

translations.literal: From after frequently exited then conquered obtained, awe shook north region.
translations.idiomatic: Thereafter he campaigned often with success and his name shook the north.

sentences.zh: 遷度遼將軍。

translations.literal: Transferred general-across-the-Liao.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to general-across-the-Liao.

sentences.zh: 耿氏自中興已後迄建安之末，大將軍二人，將軍九人，卿十三人，尚公主三人，列侯十九人，中郎將、護羌校尉及刺史、二千石數十百人，遂與漢興衰云。

translations.literal: Geng clan from middle revival after until Jian'an end, great generals two persons, generals nine persons, ministers thirteen persons, married princesses three persons, full marquises nineteen persons, palace gentlemen, protector of the Qiang colonel and governors, two thousand dan several hundred persons, thereupon with Han rise decline said.
translations.idiomatic: From the restoration of the Han to the end of the Jian'an era the house of Geng produced two grand generals, nine generals, thirteen ministers of state, three sons-in-law of the throne, nineteen full marquises, and scores of palace gentlemen, protectors of the Qiang, governors, and two-thousand-dan officials; their fortunes rose and fell with the dynasty itself.

sentences.zh: 論曰：余初讀《蘇武傳》，感其茹毛窮海，不為大漢羞。

translations.literal: Discussion says: I first read 'Su Wu zhuan', moved by his eat fur exhaust sea, not for Great Han shame.
translations.idiomatic: The appraiser writes: When I first read the 'Su Wu zhuan' I was moved that he could gnaw fur at the ends of the earth yet bring no shame on the Han.

sentences.zh: 後覽耿恭疏勒之事，喟然不覺涕之無從。

translations.literal: After viewed Gong Shule affair, sighed suddenly not aware tears' no from.
translations.idiomatic: Later, reading of Geng Gong at Shule, I sighed until the tears came unbidden.

sentences.zh: 嗟哉，義重於生，以至是乎！

translations.literal: Alas, righteousness heavier than life, to reach this!
translations.idiomatic: Alas—when duty weighs heavier than life, it comes to this!

sentences.zh: 昔曹子抗質於柯盟，相如申威於河表，蓋以決一旦之負，異乎百死之地也。

translations.literal: Formerly Cao Zi resisted hostage at Ke covenant, Xiangru extended awe beyond River, cover by decide one morning's debt, different from hundred deaths' ground.
translations.idiomatic: Of old Master Cao faced a hostage at the Ke covenant and Xiangru asserted Han majesty beyond the Yellow River—both staked a single morning's hazard, not the ground of a hundred deaths.

sentences.zh: 以為二漢當疏高爵，宥十世。

translations.literal: Thought two Han ought grant high noble rank, pardon ten generations.
translations.idiomatic: One would have thought that under the two Han such men deserved lofty titles and grace for ten generations.

sentences.zh: 而蘇君恩不及嗣，恭亦終填牢戶。

translations.literal: Yet Su lord's grace not reach heirs, Gong also finally filled prison door.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Su Wu's favor never reached his posterity, and Geng Gong too ended his days behind bars.

sentences.zh: 追誦龍蛇之章，以為嘆息。

translations.literal: Chant recite dragon snake's verse, take as sigh.
translations.idiomatic: I chant again the verse of the dragon and serpent and cannot but sigh.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：好畤經武，能畫能兵。

translations.literal: Encomium says: Haozhi practiced arms, able plan able soldier.
translations.idiomatic: The encomium runs: At Haozhi he trained for war, skilled in counsel and in arms.

sentences.zh: 往收燕卒，來集漢營。

translations.literal: Went collect Yan soldiers, came gather Han camp.
translations.idiomatic: He gathered the soldiers of Yan and brought them into Han's lines.

sentences.zh: 請間趙殿，釃酒齊城。

translations.literal: Requested private moment Zhao hall, sprinkled wine Qi city.
translations.idiomatic: He begged a word beneath the Zhao palace couch and poured the libation that took Qi.

sentences.zh: 況、舒率從，亦既有成。

translations.literal: Kuang, Shu led followed, also already had completion.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kuang and Geng Shu followed him and likewise saw their work crowned.

sentences.zh: 國圖久策，分此兇狄。

translations.literal: Guo plotted long stratagem, divided these fierce Di.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Guo laid long-range plans that split these fierce Di.

sentences.zh: 秉洽胡情，夔單虜跡。

translations.literal: Bing harmonized Hu sentiment, Kui alone traced caitiff tracks.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing read the Hu temper; Geng Kui alone tracked the raiders to their lair.

sentences.zh: 慊慊伯宗，枯泉飛液。

translations.literal: Earnest earnest Bozong, withered spring flying liquid.
translations.idiomatic: Steadfast Bozong—dry rock yielding water again.
